name: 4'-Dimethylamino-7,8-dihydroxyflavone
summary: 4'-DMA-7,8-DHF is a synthetic flavone derivative designed as a brain-derived neurotrophic factor (BDNF) mimetic for cognitive support. Evidence is extremely preliminary, with only limited in vitro and early-stage animal research; human efficacy and safety data are not yet established.
evidence_notes: No published human clinical trials located. Mechanism is based on structural analogy to 7,8-dihydroxyflavone and in vitro modeling. Real-world efficacy, bioavailability, and safety in humans remain completely unvalidated.

dosage.typical_range: 50-150 mg daily (based on supplement label recommendations; not clinically validated)
dosage.with_food: Likely beneficial due to hydrophobic nature, but not formally studied
dosage.duration_notes: Optimal duration unknown; long-term safety not established
dosage.special_populations: Pregnant/nursing women and children should avoid due to lack of safety data

common_side_effects: Unknown in humans; not formally studied

medications: Unknown; no interaction studies in humans

name: 5-HTP
summary: Direct serotonin precursor, one step closer than tryptophan. More potent for mood and appetite but requires more caution.
evidence_notes: Direct serotonin precursor

dosage.timing: Evening for sleep; divided for mood
dosage.special_populations: Depression (with supervision), sleep

name: 7,8-Dihydroxyflavone
summary: 7,8-DHF is a natural flavonoid being investigated as a potential brain-derived neurotrophic factor (BDNF) modulator with early-stage evidence in animal models. Human clinical evidence remains extremely limited, with efficacy and safety in people not yet established.
mechanism_of_action: TrkB receptor activation in preclinical models
evidence_notes: Evidence is primarily preclinical (in vitro and animal studies). No published randomized controlled trials in humans confirm efficacy or optimal dosing. Extremely limited human data; safety and bioavailability in people remain uncharacterized.

dosage.typical_range: 25–100 mg daily (typical supplement range; optimal human dose unknown)
dosage.timing: With or without food; timing studies unavailable
dosage.with_food: No established preference; fat may improve absorption but not clinically validated
dosage.duration_notes: Long-term safety in humans unknown; most user experience anecdotal
dosage.special_populations: Use not studied in pregnant, nursing, pediatric, or elderly populations; caution advised

contraindications: Use with caution in those on neurologically active medications

medications: Potential interactions with BDNF-modulating or neurotropic drugs not formally studied
supplements: No well-documented interactions; combination safety unexplored

name: Acacia Fiber
summary: Gentle prebiotic fiber from acacia gum. Very well-tolerated with slow fermentation and minimal gas.
evidence_notes: Well-tolerated prebiotic

dosage.special_populations: Sensitive digestion

name: Acai Extract
summary: Acai is a South American palm berry rich in anthocyanins and polyphenols with demonstrated antioxidant activity in vitro. Limited human clinical evidence supports specific health benefits, though preliminary studies suggest potential cardiovascular and metabolic effects.
mechanism_of_action: Anthocyanin and polyphenol-mediated free radical scavenging
evidence_notes: While acai demonstrates strong antioxidant activity in laboratory studies, human clinical trials are limited and often small. Most health claims exceed current evidence. Grade C reflects preliminary data with moderate antioxidant potential but insufficient RCT support for specific disease claims.

dosage.with_food: No specific requirement; may improve absorption with fat
dosage.duration_notes: Safe for long-term use; benefits from consistent daily intake
dosage.special_populations: Generally recognized as safe for most adults; limited data in pregnancy/lactation

common_side_effects: Minimal; rare GI upset at high doses
contraindications: None established; use with caution in pollen allergy (potential cross-reactivity)

name: Acerola Cherry Extract
summary: Acerola cherry is a tropical fruit naturally rich in vitamin C, commonly used as a whole-food vitamin C source in supplements. The standardized 25% vitamin C content provides a consistent dose of this essential micronutrient for immune support and antioxidant protection.
mechanism_of_action: Supplies additional polyphenols and bioflavonoids with antioxidant properties
Supports enzymatic and immune functions dependent on vitamin C cofactors
evidence_notes: Grade based on vitamin C's well-established benefits (Grade A evidence). Acerola as a delivery form is well-researched and bioavailable. The standardization to 25% vitamin C ensures consistent potency, though whole-food acerola extracts may offer additional phytonutrient benefits with moderate supporting evidence.

dosage.typical_range: 500–2000 mg daily (providing 125–500 mg vitamin C depending on standardization)
dosage.timing: Morning or with meals for best absorption
dosage.with_food: May be taken with or without food; absorption may be slightly enhanced with food
dosage.duration_notes: Safe for long-term daily use as a vitamin C source
dosage.special_populations: Generally safe for pregnancy and lactation when used at recommended doses; consult healthcare provider for individual needs

common_side_effects: Mild gastrointestinal upset at high doses
Diarrhea or loose stools (dose-dependent, from excess vitamin C)
contraindications: Hemochromatosis or iron overload disorders (vitamin C increases iron absorption)
Kidney disease or history of kidney stones (high vitamin C intake may increase oxalate)
G6PD deficiency (rare genetic condition; high-dose vitamin C may cause hemolysis)

medications: Warfarin and anticoagulants (very high vitamin C may interfere with INR monitoring)
Chemotherapy agents (discuss with oncologist; high-dose vitamin C may affect treatment)
supplements: Other vitamin C sources (risk of cumulative excess intake)
Iron supplements (vitamin C enhances iron absorption—may be beneficial or problematic depending on iron status)

name: Acetyl-L-Carnitine (ALCAR)
summary: Acetyl L-Carnitine is a form of carnitine that crosses the blood-brain barrier and supports mitochondrial energy production and acetylcholine synthesis. It has shown modest benefits for cognitive function and energy metabolism in some studies, with stronger evidence in elderly populations and carnitine-deficient states.
evidence_notes: Brain-penetrating carnitine

dosage.special_populations: Cognitive aging, depression

name: Activated Charcoal
summary: Porous carbon that binds toxins, gas, and some heavy metals in the GI tract, preventing absorption.
evidence_notes: Well-established for acute poisoning, popular for general detox

dosage.special_populations: Food poisoning, gas, after potential toxin exposure

serious_warnings: Can cause bowel obstruction if overused

medications: Binds almost all oral medications - separate by 2+ hours

avoid_combining_with: All medications and supplements at same time

name: Activated Charcoal (Gas/Bloating)
summary: Adsorbent for occasional gas and bloating relief, binding gas-producing compounds.
evidence_notes: Evidence for gas reduction, occasional use

dosage.timing: After gas-producing meals or as needed
dosage.with_food: After meals or away from food
dosage.special_populations: Occasional gas, bloating, food poisoning

name: Active Aging Joint Stack
summary: Comprehensive joint and mobility support for active aging: joint, collagen, and anti-inflammatory.
evidence_notes: Complete active aging support

dosage.typical_range: Joint foundation + collagen (10g) + fish oil (2g) + curcumin (500mg)
dosage.special_populations: Active adults 50+, joint maintenance

name: Acute Immune Response Stack
summary: High-potency combination for use at first sign of illness: Elderberry, Andrographis, Vitamin C, and Zinc lozenges.
evidence_notes: Evidence-based acute immune support

dosage.typical_range: Elderberry 1000mg + Andrographis 400mg 3x/day + Vitamin C 1000mg 3x/day + Zinc lozenges 75mg total/day
dosage.timing: Multiple doses throughout day, start at first symptom
dosage.with_food: With food to reduce GI upset
dosage.special_populations: Adults at first sign of cold/flu

name: Adaptogen Blend
summary: Combined adaptogen formula for comprehensive HPA axis support.
evidence_notes: Synergistic effects of combined adaptogens

dosage.special_populations: Comprehensive stress support

name: Ashwagandha + Rhodiola Stack
summary: Stress resilience and energy combination. Complementary adaptogens.
evidence_notes: Comprehensive adaptogen support

dosage.special_populations: High stress, burnout

practical_notes: Different timing - rhodiola AM, ashwagandha PM

name: Adrafinil
summary: Prodrug converting to modafinil in the liver. Unscheduled in many countries but requires liver monitoring.
evidence_notes: Prodrug - liver concerns with long-term use

dosage.special_populations: Occasional use only

practical_notes: Not for chronic use - liver strain

name: Adrenal Cortex Extract
summary: Glandular extract from animal adrenals used for adrenal fatigue support, though evidence is limited.
evidence_notes: Limited evidence, 'adrenal fatigue' not recognized by conventional medicine

dosage.duration_notes: Short-term support while addressing root causes
dosage.special_populations: Those with chronic fatigue attributed to stress

name: Adrenal Support Stack
summary: Comprehensive adrenal support: Adaptogens (Rhodiola, Ashwagandha), B5, Vitamin C, Holy Basil.
evidence_notes: Evidence-based comprehensive adrenal support

dosage.typical_range: Rhodiola 200-400mg AM + Ashwagandha 300-600mg PM + B5 250-500mg + Vitamin C 500-1000mg + Holy Basil 300mg
dosage.special_populations: Chronic stress, burnout, adrenal support

serious_warnings: Address root causes of stress too

name: Aframomum melegueta Extract
summary: Aframomum melegueta is a West African spice containing 6-paradol and related pungent compounds that may modestly support thermogenesis and metabolic rate. Evidence is preliminary and limited to animal studies and small human trials.
evidence_notes: Evidence is limited to animal studies and a small number of preliminary human trials. 6-Paradol shows mechanistic promise via thermogenic pathways but lacks robust RCT data in humans. No large-scale clinical trials have established clear efficacy for weight management.

dosage.duration_notes: Short-term safety established; long-term human safety data limited
dosage.special_populations: Pregnancy/nursing: insufficient data; caution advised

common_side_effects: Rare; potential for mild GI stimulation or oral pungency

name: African Mango Extract (Irvingia gabonensis)
summary: African mango extract from seeds has been studied for potential effects on body weight and lipid metabolism, with limited but promising preliminary clinical evidence. Most research is small-scale and of moderate quality, warranting further investigation before strong claims can be made.
mechanism_of_action: May increase satiety and reduce appetite
Possible modulation of adipokines and glucose metabolism
evidence_notes: Limited clinical RCTs (mostly small, 8-10 week duration) show modest weight and lipid benefits, but study quality and generalizability are moderate. More rigorous, longer-term research needed. No strong mechanistic data in humans.

dosage.with_food: Often taken before meals; check individual product instructions
dosage.duration_notes: Most clinical studies used 8–10 weeks; long-term safety and efficacy data are limited
dosage.special_populations: Safety in pregnancy, nursing, and children not well established; consult healthcare provider

contraindications: Known allergy to Irvingia gabonensis or related plants

medications: Potential interactions with diabetes medications—monitor blood sugar if taking antidiabetic drugs
supplements: No major documented interactions with common supplements, but limited data available

name: Agarikon
summary: Agarikon is a rare polypore fungus with deep roots in traditional European and Asian medicine for respiratory and immune health. Modern scientific evidence is sparse, limited primarily to in vitro studies.
mechanism_of_action: Traditional use pattern suggests multiple bioactive compounds
evidence_notes: Very limited human clinical research. Historical use in traditional medicine well-documented, but modern evidence primarily in vitro. Mycelium on rice biomass form not specifically studied. Endangered/rare species raises sourcing questions.

dosage.duration_notes: Traditional use continuous; optimal duration for modern supplementation unknown
dosage.special_populations: Pregnancy/nursing: no safety data; avoid or consult practitioner

common_side_effects: Minimal safety data; generally assumed well-tolerated based on historical use

medications: No well-documented interactions; consult practitioner if taking respiratory medications
supplements: Limited information; may have additive effects with other immune or respiratory-supporting mushrooms

name: Agmatine Sulfate
summary: Arginine metabolite with nitric oxide and pain-modulating effects. Enhances pumps.
evidence_notes: Emerging research, promising for pumps and mood

dosage.special_populations: Pump seekers, neuropathic pain

name: Organic Alfalfa Grass Powder
summary: Alfalfa grass powder is a nutrient-dense plant source containing vitamins, minerals, and phytochemicals traditionally used for general wellness and nutritional support. Scientific evidence supporting specific health claims remains limited and mostly preliminary.
mechanism_of_action: Rich in micronutrients (vitamins K, A, C, minerals)
Contains saponins and other bioactive compounds
evidence_notes: Limited human clinical trials; mostly traditional use and in vitro/animal studies. Nutrient composition is well-documented, but efficacy for specific health claims lacks robust RCT evidence.

dosage.timing: Any time of day; can be mixed into beverages or food
dosage.with_food: No specific requirement; may be taken with or without food
dosage.duration_notes: Can be used long-term as a nutritional supplement
dosage.special_populations: Pregnant/nursing women should consult healthcare provider; may interact with anticoagulants

common_side_effects: Rare: gastrointestinal upset in sensitive individuals
contraindications: Individuals taking anticoagulant medications (warfarin, aspirin) - alfalfa is high in vitamin K
History of lupus or autoimmune conditions (potential immunostimulatory effects)

medications: Anticoagulants/antithrombotics (high vitamin K content may reduce efficacy)

name: Algal Oil
summary: Algal oil is a plant-based source of omega-3 fatty acids (EPA and DHA) derived from microalgae, offering a vegetarian alternative to fish oil. It provides bioavailable long-chain omega-3s without fish-related contaminants or sustainability concerns.
mechanism_of_action: Provides EPA and DHA which incorporate into cell membranes
Improves endothelial function and blood flow
Supports neural cell integrity and synaptic plasticity
evidence_notes: Algal oil has good evidence for bioavailability and effectiveness comparable to fish oil in raising EPA/DHA levels. Multiple RCTs support cardiovascular and cognitive benefits, though fewer studies exist than for fish oil. Standardization and source variation affect quality.

dosage.with_food: Take with food containing fat for optimal absorption
dosage.duration_notes: Consistent daily use for 4-12 weeks typically needed to observe cardiovascular or cognitive effects
dosage.special_populations: Safe during pregnancy/lactation when providing adequate DHA (typically 200-300 mg DHA/day); appropriate for vegetarians and vegans

common_side_effects: Mild fishy aftertaste (less common than fish oil)
contraindications: Caution with anticoagulant/antiplatelet medications at very high doses (>3 g EPA+DHA daily)
Fish or shellfish allergies (though algal oil avoids this issue)

medications: Warfarin, aspirin, clopidogrel: possible additive bleeding risk at high doses; monitor INR/bleeding signs
supplements: Other omega-3 sources (fish oil, flax): avoid concurrent high-dose use to prevent excessive EPA/DHA intake
Ginkgo, garlic, curcumin: theoretical additive blood-thinning effects

name: Algal Oil (Vegan DHA)
summary: Plant-based omega-3 from microalgae. Vegan source of preformed DHA and sometimes EPA.
evidence_notes: Bioequivalent to fish DHA
mechanism_of_action: Same DHA fish get from algae

dosage.special_populations: Vegans, fish allergy

citations.title: DHA and brain health
citations.finding: DHA essential for brain structure and function

citations.title: Algal oil bioequivalence
citations.finding: Algal oil DHA bioequivalent to fish oil

name: Aloe Vera
summary: Aloe vera extract contains bioactive compounds including polysaccharides, anthraquinones, and phenolic acids with traditional use in digestive and skin health. Clinical evidence is limited and mixed, with some studies supporting laxative effects but stronger data needed for most health claims.
evidence_notes: Traditional use with some research

dosage.typical_range: 1-2 oz juice or 50-200mg gel extract daily
dosage.special_populations: Digestive inflammation, GERD

serious_warnings: Whole leaf contains laxative - use inner leaf only

name: Aloe Vera (Inner Leaf Gel)
summary: Soothing gel that supports gut lining, reduces inflammation, and promotes healing in the digestive tract.
evidence_notes: Traditional use with supporting research

dosage.typical_range: 50-200mg inner leaf gel or 1-2 oz juice 1-2x daily
dosage.duration_notes: Short to medium term for healing
dosage.special_populations: Ulcers, GERD, IBD, constipation

serious_warnings: Whole leaf contains aloin laxative - avoid long-term

medications: May affect absorption of some drugs

name: Aloe Vera (Oral)
summary: Gel from aloe plant used for digestive soothing and hydration support.
evidence_notes: Traditional digestive support

dosage.special_populations: Digestive soothing, heartburn

name: Alpha-Galactosidase
summary: Enzyme for breaking down gas-producing sugars in beans and cruciferous vegetables.
evidence_notes: Well-established for bean-related gas

dosage.special_populations: Bean/cruciferous eaters

practical_notes: Take with first bite of beans

citations.title: Alpha-galactosidase for gas and bloating
citations.finding: Reduces gas from legumes and vegetables

citations.title: Beano efficacy study
citations.finding: Alpha-galactosidase reduces flatulence from high-fiber foods

name: Alpha-GPC
summary: Highly bioavailable choline source crossing the blood-brain barrier. Supports acetylcholine and growth hormone.
evidence_notes: Superior choline delivery to brain

dosage.special_populations: Athletes, cognitive support

name: AKG (Alpha-Ketoglutarate)
summary: AKG is a metabolic intermediate in the citric acid cycle that may support muscle protein synthesis and ammonia detoxification. Evidence for performance and body composition benefits in humans remains preliminary and limited.
evidence_notes: Promising longevity research, particularly Ca-AKG

dosage.special_populations: Longevity, bone health, energy

practical_notes: Ca-AKG showed lifespan extension in mice

name: Alpha-Lipoic Acid (ALA)
summary: Universal antioxidant working in both water and fat. Regenerates other antioxidants and supports blood sugar metabolism.
mechanism_of_action: Works in water and fat compartments
evidence_notes: Well-studied for neuropathy and blood sugar

dosage.timing: Empty stomach for blood sugar; any for antioxidant
dosage.special_populations: Diabetics, neuropathy

practical_notes: R-form is natural and more potent

name: Amadou
summary: Amadou is a polypore fungus traditionally used in European folk medicine for immune and digestive support, though modern clinical research is extremely limited. Historical use as tinder and in traditional medicine exceeds scientific validation.
mechanism_of_action: Traditional use pattern suggests multiple bioactive components
evidence_notes: Virtually no human clinical trials. In vitro studies suggest antimicrobial and immune properties, but evidence is minimal and inconsistent. Mycelium on rice biomass form unstudied. Far less researched than Asian medicinal mushrooms.

dosage.duration_notes: Traditional use continuous; optimal duration for supplementation unknown
dosage.special_populations: Pregnancy/nursing: no safety data; consult practitioner

supplements: Limited information available; potential additive effects with other immune-supporting mushrooms

name: American Ginseng
summary: American ginseng is a traditional herbal adaptogen used to support energy, immune function, and stress resilience. Moderate evidence supports benefits for fatigue and immune support, though effects are generally modest and more research is needed.
mechanism_of_action: Different ginsenoside profile than Panax ginseng
evidence_notes: Gentler alternative to Korean ginseng

dosage.special_populations: Focus, blood sugar

serious_warnings: May enhance hypoglycemia with diabetes drugs
Stimulant effects - may affect sleep and anxiety
contraindications: MAOIs or within 14 days of stopping

drug_interactions.effect: May enhance blood sugar lowering; hypoglycemia risk
May decrease warfarin effect (Panax) or increase bleeding (American)
May increase stimulant effects, insomnia, anxiety
Ginseng may raise blood pressure slightly

safety.interaction_summary: Ginseng has mild stimulant and hypoglycemic effects. Main concerns: blood sugar medications, MAOIs, stimulants, and possibly warfarin. Different ginseng species may have different effects.
safety.interaction_warning: Use caution with diabetes medications (monitor blood sugar), MAOIs, and stimulants. Discontinue before surgery.
safety.species_note: Panax ginseng (Asian/Korean) is more stimulating; American ginseng is milder. Effects may differ.

name: Amla Powder (Indian Gooseberry)
summary: Amla is a traditional Ayurvedic fruit rich in vitamin C and polyphenols with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Limited clinical evidence supports potential benefits for immune function and oxidative stress, though human studies remain sparse.
mechanism_of_action: High vitamin C content and free radical scavenging
evidence_notes: Amla contains well-documented bioactive compounds (vitamin C, tannins, polyphenols) with in vitro antioxidant capacity. Limited human RCT data; most evidence derives from traditional use and preliminary/animal studies. Warrants B-grade consideration with more rigorous clinical trials.

dosage.with_food: May improve absorption and reduce gastrointestinal upset
dosage.duration_notes: Generally used as a long-term supplement; no established safety ceiling
dosage.special_populations: Pregnant/nursing women should consult healthcare provider before use

contraindications: Kidney stones (high oxalate content in some preparations)
Bleeding disorders (theoretical concern due to vitamin C and tannins)

medications: Anticoagulants/antiplatelet agents (theoretical interaction due to polyphenols)
Diabetes medications (amla may lower blood glucose)
supplements: Other high-dose vitamin C sources (additive effect)

name: Ampelopsis grossedentata Extract (Dihydromyricetin)
summary: Ampelopsis grossedentata extract is a plant-derived source of dihydromyricetin, a flavonoid traditionally used in Chinese medicine with emerging research on alcohol metabolism and antioxidant activity. Limited human clinical evidence exists; most studies are preclinical or conducted in animal models.
mechanism_of_action: ALDH2 enzyme inhibition (theoretically reducing acetaldehyde accumulation)
evidence_notes: Mechanism supported by in vitro and animal studies; limited high-quality human RCTs. One small human hangover study showed promise, but larger, well-controlled trials needed. Traditional use in Chinese medicine but not extensively researched in Western literature.

dosage.timing: Typically taken before or with alcohol consumption for hangover support; general use timing unclear
dosage.with_food: May be taken with or without food; no strong evidence either way
dosage.duration_notes: Acute use: single dose before alcohol. Chronic safety data limited.
dosage.special_populations: Pregnant/nursing women: insufficient data; avoid. Liver disease: consult healthcare provider.

contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to Ampelopsis or plant components

medications: Potential interaction with medications metabolized via ALDH2 or CYP enzymes (theoretical)

name: Amylase
summary: Starch-digesting enzyme. Helps break down complex carbohydrates for those with carb intolerance.
evidence_notes: Supports carbohydrate digestion
mechanism_of_action: Begins in mouth, continues in small intestine

dosage.special_populations: Carb intolerance

name: Andrographis
summary: Andrographis paniculata is a traditional Ayurvedic and Southeast Asian herb containing andrographolide as its primary bioactive compound. Research suggests potential immune-supporting and anti-inflammatory properties, though evidence in humans remains moderate.
evidence_notes: Strong clinical evidence for upper respiratory infections

dosage.duration_notes: Short-term for acute illness, cycle for maintenance
dosage.special_populations: Acute infections, immune support

safety.liver_kidney_notes: Generally safe, monitor with liver conditions

name: Andrographis Paniculata
summary: An extract derived specifically from the leaves of Andrographis paniculata, which contain the highest concentration of andrographolide compounds. Leaf extracts are considered the most potent source of andrographis' active constituents.
primary_uses: Cold and flu symptom duration reduction
mechanism_of_action: Enhanced immune cell activation and proliferation
evidence_notes: Multiple clinical trials demonstrate efficacy for reducing cold symptom duration and severity; good evidence for immune support, though most studies used crude extract or standardized andrographolide forms. The 10:1 ratio itself lacks specific pharmacokinetic data.

dosage.timing: With meals for better absorption and to minimize GI upset
dosage.with_food: Recommended; andrographis may cause mild GI distress on empty stomach
dosage.duration_notes: Safe for 3-12 weeks of continuous use; some recommend 4-week cycles with 1-2 week breaks
dosage.special_populations: Pregnancy contraindicated (traditional abortifacient use); nursing mothers should avoid. Use caution in autoimmune conditions.

common_side_effects: Mild GI upset (nausea, diarrhea, abdominal discomfort)
contraindications: Bile duct obstruction or gallbladder disease

medications: Hepatically metabolized drugs (CYP3A4, CYP2C19 potential substrate)

name: Andrographis paniculata Extract (10% Andrographolides)
summary: Andrographis extract standardized to contain 10% andrographolides, the primary active diterpenoid compounds responsible for immune and anti-inflammatory effects. This standardization provides consistent potency across batches.
evidence_notes: Strong clinical evidence for andrographolide-standardized extracts (typically 3-10% andrographolides) in reducing cold/flu duration and symptoms. Multiple RCTs support immune efficacy. Standardization to andrographolides (the primary active marker) provides more reliable clinical outcomes than concentration ratios alone.

dosage.timing: With meals; best taken 2-3x daily in divided doses
dosage.with_food: Recommended to reduce GI irritation and improve absorption
dosage.duration_notes: Evidence supports use for 4-12 weeks; traditional use suggests cycling (4-8 weeks on, 1-2 weeks off)
dosage.special_populations: Contraindicated in pregnancy and nursing. Caution in autoimmune conditions. Hepatic disease requires medical supervision.

contraindications: Active autoimmune disease (lupus, rheumatoid arthritis, etc.)

medications: Immunosuppressants (azathioprine, cyclosporine, methotrexate) — may reduce efficacy
supplements: Immune-stimulating herbs (echinacea, elderberry, astragalus) — additive effect

name: Anhydrous Caffeine
summary: Anhydrous caffeine is pure, water-free caffeine that provides rapid mental alertness and physical performance enhancement. It is the most common form of caffeine in dietary supplements due to stability and concentrated potency.
mechanism_of_action: Adenosine receptor antagonism in the CNS
evidence_notes: Extensive peer-reviewed research confirms efficacy for alertness, focus, and physical performance across hundreds of RCTs. One of the most well-studied dietary supplements.

dosage.typical_range: 100-400 mg daily, typically 3-6 mg/kg body weight
dosage.timing: Morning or early afternoon; peak effects at 30-60 minutes
dosage.with_food: Can be taken with or without food; taking with food may slightly delay absorption
dosage.duration_notes: Half-life of 3-5 hours; effects typically last 4-6 hours
dosage.special_populations: Limit to <400 mg/day; avoid in evening; reduce doses in pregnancy (consult healthcare provider), in sensitive individuals, or with anxiety disorders

name: Aniracetam
summary: Fat-soluble racetam with anxiolytic properties. Enhances creativity, verbal fluency, and reduces anxiety.
evidence_notes: Anxiolytic racetam

dosage.special_populations: Anxiety, creative work

name: Anti-Aging Hormone Support Stack
summary: Age-related hormone support: DHEA (if tested low), Pregnenolone (cautious), Adaptogens.
evidence_notes: Requires testing and practitioner guidance

dosage.typical_range: DHEA 10-25mg (if tested low) + Pregnenolone 5-10mg (optional) + Ashwagandha 300-600mg + Vitamin D 2000-5000IU
dosage.special_populations: Older adults with tested hormone deficiencies

name: Natural Anti-Anxiety Stack
summary: Gentle anxiolytic combination: L-Theanine + Magnesium + Lemon Balm.
evidence_notes: Gentle, well-tolerated combination

dosage.special_populations: Daytime anxiety

name: L-Theanine + Ashwagandha + Magnesium Stack
summary: Natural anxiety support without sedation or dependence.
evidence_notes: Natural anxiolytic stack

dosage.special_populations: Anxiety, stress

name: Apigenin
summary: Chamomile flavonoid with mild sedative and anti-anxiety effects. Binds GABA receptors.
evidence_notes: Chamomile's active compound

dosage.special_populations: Sleep, mild anxiety

name: Apple Cider Vinegar
summary: Apple cider vinegar powder is a dehydrated form of fermented apple juice containing acetic acid and other compounds. Popular in wellness contexts for potential digestive and metabolic support, though human clinical evidence remains limited.
mechanism_of_action: Acetic acid may slow gastric emptying
evidence_notes: Limited evidence, popular traditional remedy

dosage.typical_range: 1-2 tablespoons diluted in water before meals
dosage.special_populations: Those seeking digestive support

safety.liver_kidney_notes: May lower potassium at high doses

practical_notes: Always dilute - damages teeth and esophagus

name: Apple Pectin
summary: Soluble fiber from apples that supports gut health, cholesterol, and gentle regularity.
evidence_notes: Evidence for cholesterol and fiber benefits

dosage.special_populations: Cholesterol concerns, gentle fiber

name: Apple Powder (Malus pumila)
summary: Apple powder is a dehydrated fruit ingredient containing polyphenols, fiber, and vitamin C derived from apple fruit. Limited clinical evidence exists for specific health benefits when used as a standalone supplement, though observational data supports whole apple consumption for general wellness.
mechanism_of_action: Polyphenol antioxidants (quercetin, catechin, epicatechin) may reduce oxidative stress
Dietary fiber promotes healthy digestion and prebiotic effects
Vitamin C supports immune and collagen synthesis
evidence_notes: Apple powder as a supplement lacks robust clinical trials. Evidence is largely extrapolated from observational studies on whole apple consumption and isolated polyphenol compounds. Limited standardization of active constituents across commercial products.

dosage.timing: Any time of day; commonly mixed into beverages or smoothies
dosage.with_food: No specific requirement; can be taken with or without food
dosage.duration_notes: Intended for long-term daily use; no established maximum duration
dosage.special_populations: Generally safe for most populations; diabetes patients should monitor carbohydrate intake if using sweetened varieties

common_side_effects: Mild gastrointestinal discomfort if fiber intake is increased rapidly
Allergic reactions possible in those with birch pollen allergy (oral allergy syndrome)
contraindications: Apple allergy or Rosaceae family allergies
Caution in individuals taking warfarin (quercetin may have minor vitamin K content)

medications: Warfarin (polyphenols may have minor anticoagulant properties; clinical significance unclear)

name: Arabinogalactan
summary: A prebiotic soluble fiber derived from larch tree bark that supports immune function and gut health by promoting beneficial bacteria growth.
mechanism_of_action: Fermented by gut bacteria to produce short-chain fatty acids (SCFAs)
Promotes growth of beneficial Bifidobacteria and Lactobacilli
May enhance natural killer (NK) cell activity
evidence_notes: Good evidence for prebiotic effects and immune modulation; more research needed for clinical outcomes.

forms.notes: Most common form, easily dissolved in water
cons: Large doses may cause bloating initially

dosage.with_food: Can be taken with or without food
dosage.special_populations: Generally safe; start with lower doses if sensitive digestion

name: Arachidonic Acid (ARA)
summary: Omega-6 fatty acid that amplifies exercise-induced muscle signaling. Increases training response but also inflammation.
evidence_notes: Mechanism makes sense, limited but positive clinical data

dosage.special_populations: Advanced trainers, those seeking amplified response

name: L-Arginine
summary: Direct nitric oxide precursor. Less effective orally than citrulline but still used.
evidence_notes: Supports healing via NO and collagen

dosage.special_populations: Those who respond to arginine

name: Arginine AKG
summary: Arginine AKG is a salt form combining L-arginine with alpha-ketoglutarate, designed to enhance nitric oxide production and support muscle protein synthesis. Evidence for performance and cardiovascular benefits remains preliminary, with mixed results in clinical studies.
mechanism_of_action: Substrate for nitric oxide synthase (NOS), supporting endothelial nitric oxide production
Alpha-ketoglutarate provides TCA cycle intermediary to support energy metabolism
evidence_notes: L-arginine has moderate evidence for vascular function, but the AKG salt form lacks robust RCT data demonstrating superiority over standard L-arginine. Performance benefits in athletes remain inconsistent across studies.

dosage.timing: Pre-workout (30-60 minutes prior) for acute effects, or daily for chronic use
dosage.with_food: Can be taken with or without food; better tolerated with food for sensitive individuals
dosage.duration_notes: Acute studies typically 4-12 weeks; chronic nitric oxide benefits may require consistent supplementation
dosage.special_populations: Avoid or use cautiously in individuals with history of herpes simplex (arginine may promote viral replication); pregnant/nursing should consult healthcare provider

contraindications: Active herpes simplex infection (may exacerbate symptoms)
Recent myocardial infarction (use with medical supervision)
Concurrent use of erectile dysfunction medications (additive vasodilation risk)

medications: Phosphodiesterase-5 inhibitors (sildenafil, tadalafil) - increased vasodilation risk
supplements: Other NO donors (citrulline, beetroot, agmatine) - additive effects

name: Arginine Alpha-Ketoglutarate (2:1)
summary: A chelated form combining L-arginine with alpha-ketoglutarate (AKG) in a 2:1 ratio, designed to enhance nitric oxide production while supporting cellular energy metabolism. The AKG component may provide additional mitochondrial and protein synthesis benefits.
mechanism_of_action: L-arginine serves as NO synthase substrate for nitric oxide production
Alpha-ketoglutarate functions as a TCA cycle intermediate supporting energy production
AKG may enhance protein synthesis and reduce protein breakdown
Improved NO-mediated blood flow delivery to working muscles
evidence_notes: Limited direct RCT evidence specifically for AKG 2:1 form. Most data derive from standalone arginine or AKG studies. The synergistic benefit of the combination is theoretically sound but not robustly established in humans.

dosage.typical_range: 2-6 grams daily (providing 1.3-4 g arginine and 0.65-2 g AKG)
dosage.timing: 30-60 minutes pre-workout or divided with meals
dosage.with_food: May be better tolerated with food; no strong evidence for optimal timing
dosage.duration_notes: 4-8 weeks of consistent use typically required to assess benefits
dosage.special_populations: Same cautions as L-arginine; AKG generally well-tolerated

common_side_effects: Gastrointestinal upset (less common than free arginine HCl due to chelation)

supplements: Other AKG-containing supplements — additive energy metabolism support

name: Arnica Montana
summary: Arnica montana flower extract is a traditional herbal remedy commonly used topically for bruising, muscle soreness, and minor inflammation. Limited clinical evidence supports efficacy for post-exercise muscle soreness and bruise reduction, primarily from small-scale studies.
evidence_notes: Traditional use, variable evidence

dosage.special_populations: Surgery, injury, bruising

serious_warnings: Do not use on broken skin (topical)
Oral herb form is toxic (only use homeopathic dilutions)

name: Artemisinin (Sweet Wormwood)
summary: Antimicrobial compound from Artemisia annua used in SIBO and parasitic protocols.
evidence_notes: Traditional and emerging use for gut pathogens

dosage.special_populations: SIBO, suspected parasites

name: Artemisinin Extract
summary: Artemisinin is a sesquiterpene lactone compound derived from Artemisia annua (sweet wormwood) with established antimalarial properties recognized by WHO. Limited clinical evidence supports its use as a general supplement, though traditional medicine has employed it for centuries.
mechanism_of_action: Sesquiterpene lactone structure may interact with malaria parasites via free radical generation
evidence_notes: Strong RCT evidence exists for artemisinin in malaria treatment (WHO-approved), but limited clinical data for use as a general wellness supplement. Most evidence in supplement context is preliminary or derived from traditional use.

dosage.duration_notes: Not recommended for prolonged use without medical supervision; research on long-term supplementation is limited
dosage.special_populations: Contraindicated in pregnancy and lactation; avoid in children under medical guidance

name: Artichoke
summary: Liver and digestive support herb that promotes bile flow, lowers cholesterol, and protects liver cells.
evidence_notes: Good evidence for bile and cholesterol effects

dosage.special_populations: Those with digestive issues, cholesterol concerns

serious_warnings: May cause gallbladder attacks if gallstones present
safety.pregnancy_breastfeeding: Traditionally used but limited modern data

name: Artist's Conk
summary: Artist's Conk is a woody polypore mushroom traditionally used in Asian medicine for immune and respiratory support. Modern research on isolated compounds shows potential immunomodulatory effects, though human clinical data remains limited.
mechanism_of_action: β-glucans and polysaccharides may activate immune cells
Traditional use in supporting natural defenses
evidence_notes: Limited human clinical trials. In vitro and animal studies show immunomodulatory polysaccharides, but evidence in humans is preliminary. Mycelium on rice biomass form studied minimally.

dosage.with_food: Recommended; fat may enhance polysaccharide absorption
dosage.duration_notes: Typically used continuously; traditional use suggests 8+ weeks for optimal effects
dosage.special_populations: Pregnancy/nursing: insufficient safety data; consult practitioner

common_side_effects: Rare: mild GI upset or allergic reactions in sensitive individuals

medications: Potential additive effects with immunosuppressants—consult practitioner
supplements: May have additive immune effects with other medicinal mushrooms or immune-supporting supplements

name: Ascorbyl Palmitate (Vitamin C Ester)
summary: Ascorbyl palmitate is a fat-soluble form of vitamin C created by esterifying ascorbic acid with palmitic acid, designed for enhanced absorption in lipid environments. While theoretically superior for cellular penetration and stability, clinical evidence supporting distinct benefits over standard ascorbic acid remains limited.
mechanism_of_action: Provides bioavailable vitamin C through hydrolysis to ascorbic acid
Antioxidant protection via free radical scavenging
Cofactor for collagen cross-linking and synthesis
Fat-soluble delivery may enhance tissue distribution
evidence_notes: Vitamin C (ascorbic acid) has strong RCT evidence (grade A). Ascorbyl palmitate specifically is accepted as bioequivalent to ascorbic acid but lacks direct comparative human trials demonstrating superior clinical outcomes. Grade B reflects the established vitamin C evidence with moderate support for the ester form's claimed advantages.

dosage.with_food: May be taken with or without food; fat-soluble form potentially better absorbed with dietary fat
dosage.duration_notes: Safe for long-term use; vitamin C is water- and fat-soluble and excess is typically excreted
dosage.special_populations: Individuals with kidney disease or history of kidney stones should limit intake; those with G6PD deficiency should consult healthcare providers

common_side_effects: High doses may cause gastrointestinal upset (less common with ester form)
contraindications: High-dose vitamin C not recommended for those with hemochromatosis or thalassemia
Caution with history of kidney stones or severe kidney disease

medications: May reduce warfarin efficacy at very high doses (>2g daily)
Potential interference with certain chemotherapy agents (consult oncologist)
supplements: Synergistic with vitamin E and other antioxidants
May enhance iron absorption if iron supplement taken together

name: Ashwagandha
summary: Ashwagandha is a traditional Ayurvedic herb standardized for withanolides, with evidence supporting modest benefits for stress reduction, anxiety, and cortisol modulation. Multiple clinical trials demonstrate efficacy for stress-related symptoms, though effect sizes are generally moderate.
evidence_notes: Best-studied adaptogen for anxiety

dosage.special_populations: Anxiety, stress, low testosterone

avoid_combining_with: May be too calming with sedatives

practical_notes: Can affect thyroid - monitor if on meds

name: Ashwagandha Broad-Spectrum Complex
summary: Ashwagandha broad-spectrum complex is a full-plant extract from Withania somnifera that contains withanolides and other phytocompounds, designed to support stress resilience and adaptogenic function. Clinical evidence supports traditional use for stress reduction and cortisol management.
mechanism_of_action: Withanolide compounds modulate GABA and serotonin pathways
evidence_notes: Multiple RCTs demonstrate efficacy for stress/anxiety and sleep; broad-spectrum extracts show comparable benefits to standardized withanolide extracts in published studies, though standardization quality varies by manufacturer

dosage.timing: Split doses (e.g., 150-300 mg twice daily) often used in research
dosage.with_food: With or without food; with food may reduce GI upset
dosage.duration_notes: Consistent daily use for 4-8 weeks typically needed for optimal effects
dosage.special_populations: Generally safe in adults; pregnant/nursing women should consult healthcare provider before use

name: Astaxanthin
summary: Astaxanthin is a carotenoid with potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, naturally present in krill and microalgae. It crosses the blood–brain barrier and may support eye, skin, and cellular health.
evidence_notes: Exceptionally potent antioxidant

dosage.special_populations: Eye health, outdoor athletes, skin aging

practical_notes: Only carotenoid that crosses blood-retinal barrier

name: Astragalus
summary: Astragalus root extract is a traditional Chinese medicine ingredient studied for potential immune-supporting and adaptogenic properties. Evidence for human efficacy is limited and primarily from small trials, with most research conducted in Asia.
evidence_notes: Traditional use supported by preliminary research

dosage.duration_notes: Can use long-term for prevention, not during acute infection
dosage.special_populations: Those wanting immune support, elderly

serious_warnings: May stimulate immune system - avoid in autoimmune conditions
contraindications: Active infection (some traditions say don't use)
safety.pregnancy_breastfeeding: Traditional use in pregnancy, but limited modern data

avoid_combining_with: Use cautiously with other immune stimulants

name: Astragalus membranaceus Extract (Cycloastragenol 10%)
summary: Cycloastragenol is a triterpene compound derived from Astragalus membranaceus root that has been studied for potential telomerase activation and longevity support. Evidence remains preliminary with limited human clinical trials.
mechanism_of_action: Proposed telomerase activator (may support telomere length maintenance)
evidence_notes: Most evidence is from in vitro and animal studies. Human clinical trials on cycloastragenol specifically are very limited. Traditional use of Astragalus is well-documented, but isolated cycloastragenol data in humans is insufficient for higher grading.

dosage.typical_range: 10-50 mg cycloastragenol content daily (varies by product concentration)
dosage.timing: With or without food; morning or evening
dosage.with_food: Not critical; may be taken with meals for GI comfort
dosage.duration_notes: Long-term safety data in humans is limited; studies typically range 8-12 weeks
dosage.special_populations: Avoid during pregnancy/lactation; limited safety data in children

contraindications: Organ transplant recipients (may interfere with immunosuppression)

medications: Anticoagulants/antiplatelet agents (astragalus may have mild platelet effects)

name: AstraGin (Astragalus/Panax Extract)
summary: Patented blend of astragalus and panax notoginseng for enhanced nutrient absorption. Supports gut health and amino acid uptake.
evidence_notes: Patented ingredient with absorption research

dosage.special_populations: Athletes, those wanting enhanced absorption

medications: Less drug interaction concern than piperine
supplements: Enhances amino acids, creatine, many nutrients

practical_notes: Common in pre-workouts and amino products

name: ATP (Adenosine Triphosphate)
summary: Direct ATP supplementation. May enhance peak power and recovery.
evidence_notes: Emerging research, mechanism debated

dosage.special_populations: Power athletes

name: Avocado-Soy Unsaponifiables (ASU)
summary: Lipid extract that may protect cartilage and reduce need for NSAIDs in osteoarthritis.
evidence_notes: European OA treatment

dosage.special_populations: Osteoarthritis, cartilage protection

name: B-MFH
summary: Proprietary methylated B vitamin complex supporting cellular energy metabolism and methylation pathways.
evidence_notes: Contains well-researched B vitamins in methylated forms

dosage.special_populations: Well tolerated across populations

name: B-Complex (Energy Focus)
summary: Essential coenzymes for energy metabolism. Methylated forms for optimal function.
evidence_notes: Essential for energy production

dosage.special_populations: Fatigue, stress, poor diet, vegetarians

serious_warnings: B6 toxicity at high chronic doses

citations.title: B vitamins and energy metabolism
citations.finding: B vitamins essential cofactors for energy production

citations.title: B complex and fatigue
citations.finding: B vitamin supplementation reduces fatigue in deficient individuals

name: Bacopa monnieri
summary: Bacopa monnieri is a traditional Ayurvedic herb standardized for bacosides, compounds with demonstrated effects on memory consolidation and cognitive processing speed in multiple clinical trials. Evidence suggests it may support learning and mental clarity, particularly with consistent use over weeks to months.
evidence_notes: Well-studied for memory improvement

dosage.special_populations: Students, elderly, memory concerns

safety.pregnancy_breastfeeding: Traditionally used but limited modern data

name: BaCognize® Bacopa monnieri Extract
summary: BaCognize® is a standardized bacopa extract containing 12% bacopa glycosides, the primary active compounds. Clinical evidence supports its use for cognitive function and memory enhancement, particularly with long-term supplementation.
mechanism_of_action: Bacopa glycosides (bacopaside I & II) enhance neurotransmitter activity
May promote dendritic proliferation and synaptic transmission
Antioxidant and neuroprotective effects in the brain
Modulation of acetylcholine and GABA signaling
evidence_notes: Multiple well-designed RCTs demonstrate cognitive benefits, though most show effects after 8-12 weeks of use; BaCognize is a validated clinical extract with published bioavailability data

dosage.typical_range: 300-600 mg daily (providing 36-72 mg bacopa glycosides at 12% standardization)
dosage.timing: With meals; can be split into 2-3 doses
dosage.duration_notes: Typically requires 8-12 weeks of consistent use to observe meaningful cognitive benefits
dosage.special_populations: Dosing data in children and pregnant/nursing women is limited; consult healthcare provider

contraindications: Caution in individuals with seizure disorders
Those taking sedating medications should monitor interactions

medications: Anticholinergic drugs (bacopa may have mild cholinergic effects)
supplements: Other nootropic or adaptogenic herbs (monitor for cumulative effects)

name: Cognance™ (Bacopa monnieri) Extract
summary: Bacopa monnieri is a traditional Ayurvedic herb with published research supporting potential benefits for memory, cognitive function, and anxiety. The Cognance™ formulation is standardized to contain 10% Ebelin lactone, a bioactive constituent group.
mechanism_of_action: Modulation of acetylcholine and other neurotransmitters
Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects in the brain
Enhancement of dendritic density and synaptic plasticity
evidence_notes: Multiple controlled trials demonstrate improvements in memory retention and processing speed; consistent evidence for anxiety reduction in clinical populations. Standardized extracts (like those containing bacosides or ebelin lactones) show stronger evidence than unstandardized forms. Grade B reflects good-quality evidence, though human RCT data is more limited compared to common supplements.

dosage.timing: Divided into morning and afternoon doses, or as directed
dosage.with_food: Can be taken with or without food; taking with food may improve tolerability
dosage.duration_notes: Benefits typically appear after 4–12 weeks of consistent use; longer studies (8–12 weeks) show stronger cognitive effects
dosage.special_populations: Pregnancy and breastfeeding: limited safety data; avoid or consult healthcare provider

contraindications: Concurrent use with anticonvulsants (potential additive effects)

medications: Anticholinergic drugs: potential additive effects due to acetylcholine modulation
Anticonvulsants: may have additive CNS effects
Thyroid medications: theoretical interaction; monitor thyroid function
supplements: Other cognitive/memory-supporting herbs (ginkgo, Rhodiola): additive effects possible

name: Synapsa® Bacopa monnieri Extract (Whole Plant, 8% Bacosides)
summary: Bacopa monnieri is a traditional Ayurvedic herb standardized to bacosides, compounds with evidence supporting cognitive function, memory formation, and antioxidant activity. Multiple clinical trials demonstrate modest improvements in memory, learning, and attention with consistent supplementation over 8-12 weeks.
mechanism_of_action: Bacosides enhance dendritic transmission and synaptic plasticity
Promotes protein kinase activity and neural protein synthesis
Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects via free radical scavenging
May modulate acetylcholine and serotonin signaling
Protects against oxidative stress in hippocampal neurons
evidence_notes: Multiple well-designed RCTs demonstrate consistent, modest improvements in memory and cognitive tasks (particularly delayed recall) with 300-600 mg daily over 8-12 weeks. Standardization to 8% bacosides is evidence-backed. Effects are moderate and require sustained use; stronger evidence than most nootropics but not reaching A-grade due to modest effect sizes and variable study quality.

dosage.typical_range: 300-600 mg daily (providing 24-48 mg bacosides with 8% standardization)
dosage.timing: With meals or divided doses; morning preferred for cognitive support
dosage.with_food: Take with food to enhance absorption and reduce GI upset
dosage.duration_notes: Requires consistent use for 8-12 weeks minimum to observe cognitive benefits; effects accumulate over time. Optimal results typically appear after 12+ weeks of daily supplementation.
dosage.special_populations: Safe in children and elderly; no dose adjustment typically required based on age alone

contraindications: Bleeding disorders or anticoagulant therapy (may have mild anticoagulant effects)

supplements: Other cognitive/memory supplements (additive effects expected, not contraindicated)

name: Bacopa monnieri Extract (Synapsa®)
summary: Bacopa monnieri is a traditional Ayurvedic herb standardized to bacosides (active alkaloid compounds) with moderate evidence supporting cognitive and memory benefits in human studies. Synapsa® is a branded, clinically-studied extract form.
mechanism_of_action: Modulation of neurotransmitter signaling (acetylcholine, serotonin, GABA)
Enhanced dendritic spine growth and synaptic transmission
evidence_notes: Multiple randomized controlled trials demonstrate modest improvements in memory acquisition and retention with standardized bacopa extract (300-600 mg daily). Benefits typically emerge after 4-8 weeks of use. Synapsa® has dedicated clinical research supporting cognitive benefits, justifying B-grade evidence.

dosage.typical_range: 300-600 mg daily (standardized to 8% bacosides = 24-48 mg bacosides)
dosage.timing: With meals; once or twice daily
dosage.with_food: Recommended—enhances absorption and reduces GI upset
dosage.duration_notes: Consistent use for 4-8 weeks required to observe cognitive benefits; effects build over time
dosage.special_populations: Pregnancy/lactation: insufficient safety data; use caution. Individuals on anticholinergic medications should consult healthcare provider.

contraindications: Gastric or duodenal ulcers (may cause mild irritation)

medications: Anticholinergic agents (bacopa may enhance cholinergic effects)
Thyroid medications (one study noted minor interaction; monitor thyroid function)
supplements: Other cholinergic nootropics (acetylcholine-enhancing compounds may compound effects)

name: Baikal Skullcap
summary: Baikal skullcap is a traditional Chinese medicinal herb containing bioactive flavonoids (baicalin, wogonin) with preliminary evidence for anxiolytic, anti-inflammatory, and neuroprotective properties. Most human research remains limited, with effects primarily demonstrated in animal models and in vitro studies.
mechanism_of_action: Flavonoid compounds (baicalin, baicalein, wogonin) exhibit antioxidant activity
May modulate inflammatory pathways and cytokine production
evidence_notes: Substantial in vitro and animal model data supports anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects; human clinical trials are sparse and often low-quality. Traditional use is well-documented, but modern RCT evidence in humans is limited. Grade reflects moderate/preliminary human evidence.

dosage.typical_range: 500-2000 mg daily (dried root powder) or 300-600 mg standardized extract
dosage.timing: Can be taken with meals; traditionally consumed as decoction 2-3 times daily
dosage.with_food: No specific requirement; may take with or without food
dosage.duration_notes: Traditional use supports long-term consumption; optimal duration for specific benefits not established in human studies
dosage.special_populations: Pregnancy/nursing: limited safety data; use with caution. Pediatric: insufficient evidence.

medications: Potential CYP3A4 interaction (theoretical; avoid with sensitive substrates)

name: Bamboo Extract (Bambusa vulgaris)
summary: Bamboo extract is a plant-based source of bioavailable silica, a trace mineral involved in collagen synthesis and connective tissue formation. Evidence for efficacy in hair, skin, and joint health is limited and largely preliminary.
mechanism_of_action: Provides bioavailable silica for collagen cross-linking
Supports hydroxyapatite formation in bone matrix
May enhance dermal elastin and collagen synthesis
evidence_notes: Limited human clinical trials specific to bamboo extract. Most evidence is in vitro or from small observational studies. Silica's role in connective tissue is well-established biochemically, but efficacy studies on bamboo extract specifically are sparse and often industry-sponsored.

dosage.typical_range: 300–1,000 mg daily (containing 70% silica = ~210–700 mg elemental silica)
dosage.with_food: May enhance absorption when taken with food
dosage.duration_notes: Studies suggest 8–12 weeks for observable effects on hair and skin; long-term safety profile not extensively documented
dosage.special_populations: No established data for pregnancy/lactation; use cautiously in renal disease due to silica accumulation potential

common_side_effects: Rare: dry mouth or constipation at high doses

medications: May interfere with mineral absorption if taken with certain medications; separate dosing by 2 hours from iron or calcium supplements
supplements: May reduce absorption of iron, calcium, or zinc if taken simultaneously

name: Banaba Leaf Extract
summary: Banaba leaf extract from Lagerstroemia speciosa contains corosolic acid and has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine for blood sugar management. Preliminary research suggests potential glucose-regulating properties, though clinical evidence remains limited.
mechanism_of_action: Corosolic acid may enhance glucose uptake via GLUT4 translocation
evidence_notes: Several small clinical trials and in vitro studies show promise for glucose regulation, but evidence is preliminary with limited high-quality RCTs. Most data from traditional use and animal models.

dosage.typical_range: 32-48 mg corosolic acid daily (equivalent to 1,600-2,400 mg leaf extract at 2% standardization)
dosage.with_food: May be taken with or without food; with meals may optimize glucose-related effects
dosage.duration_notes: Effects may develop over weeks; long-term safety data limited
dosage.special_populations: Pregnant/nursing women should avoid; individuals on diabetes medications should consult healthcare provider

common_side_effects: Mild gastrointestinal upset reported in some users
contraindications: Concurrent use with anti-diabetic medications may increase hypoglycemia risk
Kidney or liver disease (limited safety data)

medications: Diabetes medications (metformin, sulfonylureas, insulin) — may increase hypoglycemia risk

name: Baobab Fruit Powder
summary: Baobab fruit powder is a nutrient-dense whole food ingredient rich in vitamin C, fiber, and antioxidants traditionally used in African cuisine. Limited clinical studies support claims regarding digestive health and antioxidant activity, though preliminary evidence suggests potential benefits.
mechanism_of_action: High polyphenol and antioxidant content (gallic acid, epicatechin, catechin)
Prebiotic fiber supporting beneficial gut bacteria
Vitamin C contributing to immune function
evidence_notes: Limited human clinical trials; most evidence comes from in vitro studies and small exploratory research. Nutrient composition well-established, but clinical efficacy in humans remains preliminary.

dosage.typical_range: 1-2 teaspoons (5-10 grams) daily or 2-6 grams in clinical studies
dosage.timing: Can be taken anytime; often mixed into beverages or food
dosage.with_food: Not required; can be taken with or without food
dosage.duration_notes: Safe for long-term use; traditional food ingredient
dosage.special_populations: Generally recognized as safe; no established restrictions

common_side_effects: Mild digestive effects possible (high fiber content) if intake is excessive

name: Barberry Extract (Berberis vulgaris)
summary: Barberry extract is traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Chinese medicine, primarily valued for its berberine alkaloid content which has shown antimicrobial and modest metabolic effects in research. Clinical evidence is limited, with most support derived from berberine isolated studies rather than whole barberry extract preparations.
mechanism_of_action: Berberine alkaloid acts as an antimicrobial agent
May support glucose metabolism through AMPK activation
evidence_notes: Most human clinical evidence focuses on isolated berberine rather than whole barberry extract. Traditional use is well-documented, but controlled trials specifically on barberry extract are limited. Some indirect evidence from berberine research applies, but direct clinical validation for barberry root/bark extract is moderate at best.

dosage.timing: With meals for optimal absorption and reduced GI irritation
dosage.with_food: Recommended with food to minimize gastrointestinal upset
dosage.duration_notes: Typically used for 4-12 weeks; long-term safety data limited
dosage.special_populations: Avoid during pregnancy/lactation; use cautiously in liver disease

medications: May interact with diabetes medications (hypoglycemic effect)
supplements: May have additive effects with other antimicrobial herbs

name: Barley Grass Powder
summary: Barley grass is the young leaf of the barley plant, rich in chlorophyll, vitamins, and minerals. Limited clinical evidence supports traditional claims of improved energy and detoxification.
evidence_notes: Barley grass contains beneficial nutrients and some in vitro antioxidant studies exist, but human clinical trials are sparse and most health claims lack robust RCT evidence. Traditional use is well-established but not strongly validated scientifically.

dosage.timing: Can be taken any time; often consumed in morning with water or juice
dosage.with_food: May be taken with or without food; mixing with juice or smoothies is common
dosage.duration_notes: Generally safe for long-term use; no established maximum duration
dosage.special_populations: Generally well-tolerated; pregnant/nursing women should consult healthcare provider

contraindications: Celiac disease or gluten sensitivity (though barley grass itself is typically gluten-free, cross-contamination possible)
Bleeding disorders (high vitamin K content may interact with anticoagulants)

medications: Warfarin and anticoagulants (high vitamin K content)

name: BCAAs
summary: A branched-chain amino acid blend in a 2:1:1 leucine-to-isoleucine-to-valine ratio, formulated in instantized (micronized) form for improved solubility. BCAAs support muscle protein synthesis and may reduce exercise-induced fatigue, though whole protein sources provide comparable benefits at lower cost.
evidence_notes: Superseded by EAAs or adequate protein, but still useful

dosage.special_populations: Fasted training, low protein meals

name: Bearberry Leaf Extract
summary: Bearberry leaf extract has been traditionally used to support urinary tract health, primarily due to its arbutin content which converts to hydroquinone in the body. Evidence for efficacy is limited and mixed, with most studies being small or dated.
mechanism_of_action: Arbutin metabolites may have antimicrobial properties
Possible mild antiseptic action on urinary tract tissues
evidence_notes: Traditional use for UTI support is well-documented, but modern RCT evidence is limited and inconsistent. Most available studies are small, older, or show modest benefits. Safety concerns regarding hydroquinone metabolites warrant caution.

dosage.typical_range: 400–840 mg daily (dried leaf or standardized extract)
dosage.timing: Typically divided into 2–3 doses throughout the day
dosage.with_food: May be taken with or without food; some sources recommend with meals to minimize potential GI irritation
dosage.duration_notes: Traditional use suggests 1–4 weeks of continuous use; long-term use (>4 weeks) not recommended without medical supervision due to hydroquinone accumulation concerns
dosage.special_populations: Not recommended in pregnancy, lactation, or children without professional guidance. Avoid in those with kidney disease or urinary retention.

contraindications: Kidney disease or compromised renal function
Inflammatory bowel conditions in susceptible individuals

medications: Potential interaction with medications affected by pH changes in urine
Caution with medications requiring acidic urine for efficacy
supplements: Concurrent diuretic herbs may potentiate effects

name: Bee Pollen
summary: Bee pollen is a mixture of flower pollen, nectar, enzymes, and bee secretions with a complex nutrient profile including proteins, amino acids, vitamins, and minerals. Limited clinical evidence supports its use for general wellness, though traditional and folk medicine applications are common.
mechanism_of_action: Provides bioactive compounds including polyphenols, flavonoids, and amino acids
May have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties
Potential immunomodulatory effects from diverse phytonutrients
evidence_notes: Most evidence is from in vitro or small animal studies. Few well-designed human RCTs; those available show inconsistent results for allergies and performance. Safety profile is generally good but efficacy claims are not strongly supported.

dosage.timing: Can be taken any time of day; often split into divided doses
dosage.with_food: No specific requirement; may be mixed into food or beverages
dosage.duration_notes: Intended for ongoing use; no established maximum duration
dosage.special_populations: Avoid if allergic to bee products, pollen, or honey; use with caution in individuals with pollen allergies or asthma

contraindications: Pollen allergies or severe hay fever

medications: No well-documented interactions, though polyphenols may have mild anticoagulant properties

name: Bee Propolis
summary: Bee propolis is a resinous mixture collected by honeybees with antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, commonly standardized to 5% flavonoids for consistency. Limited but promising human evidence supports its use for immune support and oral health, though most research remains preliminary.
evidence_notes: Traditional use with research supporting antimicrobial effects

dosage.special_populations: Immune support, oral health, wound healing

name: Beet
summary: Beet root powder is derived from the root of Beta vulgaris and contains bioactive compounds including betalains and nitrates, which may support cardiovascular function and exercise performance. Evidence supports benefits for blood pressure regulation and endothelial function, particularly in nitrate-sensitive contexts.
evidence_notes: Proven nitric oxide support

dosage.special_populations: Blood pressure, athletes, exercise performance

name: Beetroot / Nitrates
summary: Dietary nitrates converted to nitric oxide for improved endurance and blood flow. Can enhance exercise efficiency.
evidence_notes: Well-studied for endurance

dosage.special_populations: Endurance athletes

practical_notes: Don't use mouthwash before - kills needed bacteria

citations.title: Beetroot and exercise performance
citations.finding: Beetroot juice improves exercise efficiency and time to exhaustion

citations.title: Nitrate supplementation meta-analysis
citations.finding: Dietary nitrates improve exercise performance

name: Benfotiamine
summary: Fat-soluble thiamine derivative with superior bioavailability. Particularly effective for diabetic neuropathy and AGE reduction.
evidence_notes: Superior bioavailability for neuropathy

dosage.special_populations: Diabetics, neuropathy

practical_notes: Superior to regular thiamine for neuropathy

name: Bentonite Clay (Internal)
summary: Adsorbent clay used internally for occasional detox and digestive support.
evidence_notes: Traditional use, limited clinical evidence

dosage.typical_range: 1 tsp in water or as directed, occasional use
dosage.special_populations: Occasional detox

name: Berberine
summary: Berberine is an alkaloid compound from Berberis aristata with established clinical evidence for supporting glucose metabolism and cardiovascular health markers. The Phytosome® delivery system enhances bioavailability compared to standard berberine.
evidence_notes: Strong evidence, comparable to metformin

dosage.special_populations: Prediabetes, metabolic syndrome, PCOS

upper_limit.label: No established UL
upper_limit.notes: Many drug interactions — check with provider

serious_warnings: Can cause hypoglycemia with diabetes meds

practical_notes: Monitor blood sugar if on meds

name: Bergamot Extract
summary: Citrus extract with statin-like effects on cholesterol. Supports lipid profile.
evidence_notes: Natural lipid support

dosage.special_populations: Mild lipid elevation, statin-intolerant

name: Beta-Alanine
summary: Increases muscle carnosine for buffering acid during high-intensity exercise. Delays fatigue in 1-4 minute efforts.
evidence_notes: Proven for high-intensity exercise

dosage.timing: Any time, consistency matters; split doses reduce tingling
dosage.special_populations: Athletes in 1-4 min events

citations.title: Beta-alanine ISSN position stand
citations.finding: Improves exercise capacity, especially 1-4 minute efforts

citations.title: Beta-alanine meta-analysis
citations.finding: Significant improvement in exercise performance

name: Beta-Carotene
summary: Provitamin A carotenoid converted to retinol as needed. Cannot cause toxicity. Provides independent antioxidant benefits.
evidence_notes: Safe vitamin A source

dosage.special_populations: Smokers avoid high-dose supplements

name: Beta Ecdysterone
summary: Beta ecdysterone is a plant-derived steroid hormone analog studied for potential muscle-building and athletic performance effects. Evidence for human efficacy remains limited, with most studies showing modest improvements in strength or body composition, though results are inconsistent.
mechanism_of_action: Potential anabolic signaling via non-androgenic pathways
Reported muscle fiber growth stimulation in animal models
evidence_notes: Limited human RCT data with mixed results. Some studies suggest modest benefits for strength or muscle mass, but effect sizes are generally small and findings are not consistently replicated. Animal and in vitro data are more promising but do not reliably translate to humans.

dosage.timing: Distributed throughout the day with meals
dosage.special_populations: Safety in athletes subject to drug testing standards should be verified; limited data in women and younger populations

common_side_effects: Rare reports of mild gastrointestinal upset

medications: Potential interactions with hormonal medications (theoretical; limited data)
supplements: No major documented interactions with common supplements

name: Beta Glucan
summary: Beta glucan is a soluble polysaccharide found in yeast, mushrooms, and grains that supports immune function by activating immune cells. Multiple clinical studies demonstrate modest benefits for immune response and upper respiratory health.
mechanism_of_action: binds to pattern recognition receptors on immune cells
evidence_notes: Strong evidence for immune modulation

dosage.with_food: can be taken with or without food
dosage.duration_notes: benefits typically appear after 4-8 weeks of consistent use
dosage.special_populations: Generally well-tolerated across age groups; immunocompromised individuals should consult healthcare provider

common_side_effects: rare: allergic reactions in yeast-sensitive individuals

name: Beta Glucan 1,3/1,6 (from Saccharomyces cerevisiae)
summary: Beta-glucan 1,3/1,6 from baker's yeast is a soluble polysaccharide that may support immune function by enhancing natural killer cell activity and promoting a healthy inflammatory response. Moderate clinical evidence supports its use for general immune support, particularly during periods of stress or seasonal challenges.
mechanism_of_action: Binds to pattern recognition receptors (Dectin-1) on innate immune cells
Stimulates macrophage and natural killer cell activity
evidence_notes: Multiple RCTs demonstrate immune-supportive effects, particularly for NK cell activity and respiratory health in stressed populations. However, variation in extraction methods and dosing protocols limits generalizability; most robust data exists for specific yeast beta-glucan preparations.

dosage.timing: With meals or as directed on label
dosage.with_food: May enhance absorption; typically taken with food
dosage.duration_notes: Consistent daily use for 4+ weeks may be needed for optimal immune support effects
dosage.special_populations: Generally recognized as safe for most adults; limited data in pregnancy/lactation

common_side_effects: Rare mild gastrointestinal effects (nausea, digestive changes)
contraindications: Severe immunocompromised states (immunosuppressive therapy) — may cause excessive immune activation; consult healthcare provider
Known baker's yeast allergy or sensitivity

medications: Immunosuppressive drugs (corticosteroids, transplant medications) — potential for conflicting immune effects; use only under medical supervision

name: Beta-Glucan Fiber
summary: Soluble fiber from oats or mushrooms with immune-modulating and cholesterol-lowering effects.
evidence_notes: FDA health claim for cholesterol

dosage.typical_range: 3 g daily for cholesterol; 250-500mg mushroom for immune
dosage.special_populations: Cholesterol concerns, immune support

practical_notes: Oat for cholesterol, mushroom for immune

citations.title: Beta-glucan and cholesterol
citations.finding: 3g/day reduces LDL cholesterol by 5-10%

citations.title: Beta-glucan and immune function
citations.finding: Enhances immune response and reduces infection risk

name: Beta-Hydroxybutyrate (BHB)
summary: Exogenous ketone body that can elevate blood ketone levels. Used for energy, cognitive function, and supporting ketogenic diets.
evidence_notes: Good evidence for raising ketone levels; cognitive and performance benefits still emerging
mechanism_of_action: Provides alternative fuel source for brain and muscles
Bypasses need for dietary carb restriction

dosage.special_populations: Keto dieters, athletes

practical_notes: Effects are temporary - ketones clear in hours
Not a replacement for ketogenic diet

name: Beta Sitosterol
summary: Beta sitosterol is a plant sterol that helps reduce LDL cholesterol absorption in the intestines, with consistent evidence supporting modest cholesterol-lowering effects. Daily intake of 1.5-2.4 g has been shown to reduce LDL cholesterol by approximately 10% in clinical studies.
mechanism_of_action: Competitive inhibition of dietary and biliary cholesterol absorption in the small intestine
Displacement of cholesterol in mixed micelles
evidence_notes: Strong evidence for BPH urinary symptoms

forms.form: From saw palmetto or other sources

dosage.with_food: Required; take with fat-containing meals to enhance efficacy
dosage.duration_notes: Benefits typically appear after 2-4 weeks of consistent use; long-term use is safe
dosage.special_populations: Safe for most adults; limited data in children and pregnancy

common_side_effects: Occasional mild gastrointestinal symptoms at high doses

medications: May reduce absorption of fat-soluble vitamins (A, D, E, K) at very high doses
May have additive effects with statin medications
supplements: Ensure adequate vitamin D and K intake with regular use

name: Betaine HCl
summary: Supplemental stomach acid for those with low acid production. Supports protein digestion and mineral absorption.
evidence_notes: Addresses hypochlorhydria

dosage.special_populations: Over 50, PPI users

serious_warnings: Don't use with ulcers or gastritis

practical_notes: Start low and increase until warmth

name: Betaine (TMG)
summary: Methyl donor supporting strength, power, and body composition. Also important for homocysteine metabolism.
evidence_notes: Emerging performance benefits

dosage.special_populations: Athletes, high homocysteine

upper_limit.label: Body odor at very high doses

common_side_effects: Fishy body odor at high doses

supplements: Works with B12, folate for methylation

name: Bifidobacterium breve
summary: A probiotic strain commonly found in the infant gut microbiome, supporting digestive health, immune function, and skin health.
evidence_notes: Multiple clinical studies in pediatric and adult populations
mechanism_of_action: Colonizes the gut and competes with pathogens

dosage.special_populations: Well studied in pediatric populations

supplements: Synergistic with other probiotics and prebiotics

name: Bifidobacterium infantis 35624
summary: Most-studied probiotic strain for IBS. Repeated RCTs show symptom improvement at 1 billion CFU/day.
evidence_notes: Multiple RCTs in IBS show reduced abdominal pain, bloating, and urgency. Effect is strain-specific — generic B. infantis products may not deliver the same benefit.
mechanism_of_action: Modulates intestinal cytokine balance (raises IL-10, lowers IL-6/TNF-alpha)
Restores gut barrier function in inflammatory states

forms.notes: Marketed as Align in many regions
Common but evidence is for the standalone strain

dosage.with_food: With or without food per product directions
dosage.duration_notes: Trials show benefit emerging over 4 weeks; safe for long-term use
dosage.special_populations: Generally safe in pregnancy and pediatrics; consult provider in immunocompromised states

common_side_effects: Transient gas or bloating in the first 1-2 weeks
serious_warnings: Avoid in severely immunocompromised patients due to rare bacteremia risk with any probiotic
contraindications: Central venous catheters in critically ill patients

supplements: Synergistic with prebiotics (inulin, GOS) but not required

avoid_combining_with: Antibiotics taken at the same time

practical_notes: Strain matters — generic B. infantis is not equivalent
Most IBS RCT data is for IBS-D and mixed-type

name: Bifidobacterium lactis
summary: Hardy probiotic strain with strong immune-enhancing effects. Survives transit well and supports regularity.
evidence_notes: Robust strain with immune benefits

dosage.special_populations: Immune support, constipation

name: Bifidobacterium longum
summary: Dominant infant gut bacteria that supports immune function and may reduce anxiety. Important throughout life.
evidence_notes: Important from infancy through aging

dosage.special_populations: Stress, immune support

citations.title: B. longum and mental health
citations.finding: Reduces stress and improves mood in healthy adults

citations.title: Probiotics meta-analysis
citations.finding: Effective for various GI conditions

name: Bilberry Extract
