contraindications: IBS-D (irritable bowel syndrome with diarrhea) — may exacerbate symptoms
Small intestinal bacterial overgrowth (SIBO) — high FODMAP content
Pregnancy and lactation — limited safety data, use with caution

supplements: May interact with or potentiate effects of other prebiotics or probiotics (not problematic; often used synergistically)

name: Chitosan
summary: Chitosan is a polysaccharide fiber derived from shellfish exoskeletons that has been studied for lipid management and weight support. Evidence for meaningful clinical benefit is moderate at best, with mixed results in human trials.
mechanism_of_action: Binds dietary lipids and cholesterol in the gastrointestinal tract
evidence_notes: Some human studies show modest effects on cholesterol and weight, but results are inconsistent and effect sizes are small. Most positive data come from older trials; more recent rigorous RCTs show limited benefit. Not strong enough for grade B.

dosage.timing: With meals, particularly meals containing fat
dosage.with_food: Take with food for intended lipid-binding effect
dosage.duration_notes: Typically used as ongoing supplement; studies range 4–24 weeks
dosage.special_populations: Shellfish allergy is a contraindication; caution in those with seafood sensitivities

contraindications: Caution with history of bowel obstruction

medications: May reduce absorption of fat-soluble vitamins (A, D, E, K) if taken concurrently
Potential interaction with lipid-lowering medications (additive effect possible)
supplements: May reduce absorption of fat-soluble supplement vitamins if taken together

name: Chlorella
summary: Chlorella is a nutrient-dense freshwater green algae containing protein, B vitamins, minerals, and chlorophyll. Evidence for specific health benefits remains limited, though it may support detoxification and overall nutritional intake.
evidence_notes: Traditional use with research on detox and nutrient content

dosage.special_populations: Detox support, nutrient density

practical_notes: Broken cell wall form better absorbed
Start low to avoid detox symptoms

name: Chloride
summary: Essential electrolyte mineral that works with sodium and potassium to maintain fluid balance, nerve transmission, and stomach acid production.
evidence_notes: Essential mineral with well-established physiological roles
mechanism_of_action: Works with sodium to maintain osmotic pressure
Essential for carbon dioxide transport in blood

dosage.special_populations: Athletes may need more during exercise

serious_warnings: Excess can raise blood pressure (with sodium)

supplements: Part of electrolyte balance with Na/K

name: Choline (Various Forms)
summary: Essential nutrient for brain, liver, and methylation. Most people don't get enough. Available as various forms with different benefits.
evidence_notes: Essential nutrient, many people deficient

dosage.special_populations: Cognitive support, liver health, pregnancy

common_side_effects: Fishy body odor at high doses
serious_warnings: Very high doses may increase TMAO (cardiovascular concern)

supplements: Works with B vitamins for methylation

name: Choline
summary: An essential nutrient required for the synthesis of acetylcholine, phospholipids (including phosphatidylcholine), and betaine, which play crucial roles in cognitive function, cell membrane integrity, and methylation reactions. Choline bitartrate is a common supplemental form.
evidence_notes: Good evidence for choline's role in cognitive function and neurotransmitter synthesis in deficient populations. Moderate RCT evidence in healthy adults.

dosage.timing: Can be taken anytime; morning dosing may support daytime cognitive function
dosage.with_food: Can be taken with or without food
dosage.duration_notes: Adequate Intake (AI) is 425-550 mg daily; long-term use is safe
dosage.special_populations: Pregnant/lactating women: 450-550 mg daily; evidence suggests prenatal choline supports fetal brain development

common_side_effects: Fishy body odor (due to trimethylamine production)

medications: No major documented interactions at normal doses

name: Choline (as Choline Chloride)
summary: Choline is an essential nutrient required for acetylcholine synthesis, cell membrane integrity, and methyl-group donation via the phosphatidylcholine pathway. It is recognized as an essential nutrient by the National Academy of Medicine with established dietary requirements.
mechanism_of_action: Component of phosphatidylcholine in cell membranes
evidence_notes: Choline is an established essential nutrient with well-characterized roles in cognition and liver health. Good evidence supports benefits for memory and cognitive function in deficient populations. Most studies use doses of 400–2000 mg daily. Evidence for cognitive enhancement in replete individuals is more modest (grade B rather than A).

dosage.timing: Can be taken any time of day; no specific timing requirement
dosage.with_food: May be taken with or without food; absorption may be slightly enhanced with food
dosage.duration_notes: Safe for long-term daily use; benefits develop over weeks to months
dosage.special_populations: Pregnant and nursing women have higher requirements (450–550 mg daily). Adequate intake for non-pregnant adults is 425 mg (women) / 550 mg (men)

common_side_effects: Fishy body odor (in high doses, related to TMAO production)
contraindications: Individuals with trimethylaminuria (TMAU) should avoid high doses
Caution in those with family history of cardiovascular disease (high TMAO levels may be concerning)

medications: Potential interaction with anticholinergic drugs (theoretical, monitor for additive effects)
supplements: Betaine and choline may have additive effects on methyl-group donation
B vitamins (B12, folate, B6) support choline's one-carbon metabolism role

name: Choline (as DL-Choline Bitartrate)
summary: Choline (as DL-choline bitartrate) is an essential nutrient required for neurotransmitter synthesis, cell membrane integrity, and lipid metabolism. Bitartrate is an inexpensive salt form with moderate bioavailability.
evidence_notes: Choline has good evidence for liver and cognitive health; bitartrate form has moderate bioavailability; more bioavailable forms include CDP-choline and choline alphoscerate.

dosage.timing: can be taken anytime; typically with meals for GI tolerance
dosage.with_food: Taking with food may improve tolerance and absorption
dosage.duration_notes: Safe for long-term use; adequate intake 550 mg (men), 425 mg (women)
dosage.special_populations: Pregnant/lactating women: 930 mg daily; DL form has lower activity than L-form

name: Chondroitin Sulfate
summary: Major cartilage component. Often combined with glucosamine for comprehensive joint support.
evidence_notes: Often combined with glucosamine

dosage.special_populations: OA, joint wear

citations.title: Chondroitin sulfate meta-analysis
citations.finding: Small but significant effect on pain and function in OA

citations.title: Chondroitin structure-modifying effects
citations.finding: May slow structural progression of knee OA

name: Chromium
summary: Essential trace mineral for insulin function. May improve glucose uptake.
evidence_notes: Essential for insulin function

dosage.special_populations: Blood sugar concerns, carb cravings

supplements: Works with other blood sugar support

practical_notes: May help some with insulin resistance

name: Chromium (as Chromium Chloride)
summary: Chromium is a trace mineral that enhances insulin action and glucose metabolism as part of the glucose tolerance factor (GTF). It plays a supportive role in macronutrient metabolism and blood sugar regulation.
primary_uses: Enhances insulin sensitivity and glucose tolerance
May help stabilize blood sugar levels
mechanism_of_action: Potentiates insulin receptor signaling and glucose uptake
Component of glucose tolerance factor (GTF) with niacin and amino acids
Enhances GLUT4 translocation to cell membrane
evidence_notes: Chromium has solid mechanistic support and several RCTs demonstrating modest effects on glucose control and insulin sensitivity, though effect sizes are inconsistent. Evidence for healthy individuals is weaker than for those with dysglycemia.

dosage.with_food: Recommended; enhances absorption and optimizes glucose metabolism context
dosage.duration_notes: Studies show effects develop over weeks to months; long-term safety is generally good at recommended doses
dosage.special_populations: Caution in patients with renal impairment; those with insulin-dependent diabetes should monitor glucose under medical supervision

common_side_effects: Rare: headache, insomnia, or mood changes reported at higher doses
contraindications: Severe renal impairment (chromium is renally cleared)
Concurrent use of corticosteroids (which increase chromium excretion and may reduce efficacy)

medications: Insulin and oral hypoglycemic agents (may enhance effect; monitor glucose)
NSAIDs and corticosteroids (may increase chromium losses)
supplements: Niacin (may synergize glucose tolerance factor activity)
High-dose vitamin C (may increase chromium absorption and excretion)

name: Chromium (as Chromium Nicotinate Glycinate Chelate)
summary: Chromium is a trace mineral that enhances insulin signaling and glucose metabolism by potentiating insulin receptor activity. The nicotinate glycinate chelate form combines chromium with niacin and glycine for improved stability and absorption.
mechanism_of_action: Enhances insulin receptor tyrosine kinase activity
evidence_notes: Good evidence for chromium supporting glucose control in insulin resistance and diabetes. Nicotinate glycinate chelate form has reasonable bioavailability data, though direct comparative evidence vs. other forms is limited.

dosage.duration_notes: Safe for long-term use at these doses
dosage.special_populations: Diabetes: consult healthcare provider; may require medication adjustment. Renal disease: use with caution.

contraindications: Concurrent chromium supplementation from multiple sources

medications: May enhance insulin/oral hypoglycemic drugs—monitor blood glucose
supplements: Iron, zinc, calcium may compete for absorption

name: Chromium Picolinate
summary: Trace mineral for insulin sensitivity and blood sugar regulation. Popular for carb cravings.
evidence_notes: Modest but consistent blood sugar benefits

dosage.special_populations: Diabetics monitor blood sugar

practical_notes: Most effective in deficiency or diabetes

name: Cilantro Extract (Coriandrum sativum)
summary: Herb that may help mobilize heavy metals from tissues, often used with chlorella for binding.
evidence_notes: Preliminary evidence, often used traditionally
mechanism_of_action: May help mobilize mercury from tissues

dosage.typical_range: 500-1000mg extract or several drops tincture daily
dosage.special_populations: Those with heavy metal concerns, post-amalgam removal

serious_warnings: May mobilize metals without proper binding
contraindications: Don't use alone for heavy metal detox

avoid_combining_with: Don't use alone for metal detox

practical_notes: Always use with binder like chlorella
Can redistribute metals if used alone

name: Cinnamon Extract (Ceylon)
summary: Ceylon cinnamon may support healthy blood sugar levels. Lower coumarin than cassia.
evidence_notes: Modest blood sugar support

dosage.special_populations: Prediabetes, blood sugar management

supplements: Works with other blood sugar support

name: Cissus Quadrangularis Extract
summary: Cissus quadrangularis is a traditional plant extract standardized to ketosterones, primarily studied for bone health and joint support in both animal and limited human research. Evidence remains preliminary, with most human studies showing modest effects on bone metabolism and fracture healing.
mechanism_of_action: Ketosterone compounds may modulate bone-specific alkaline phosphatase and osteogenic pathways
Potential anti-inflammatory effects on joint tissue
May support collagen synthesis in connective tissue
evidence_notes: Limited human clinical trial data; most evidence comes from animal studies and a small number of human studies with modest sample sizes. Some positive findings for bone metabolism markers and fracture healing, but larger, well-controlled RCTs are needed. Standardization to ketosterones is appropriate but research is not extensive.

dosage.with_food: Recommended with food for better absorption
dosage.duration_notes: Studies typically evaluated 4-12 weeks; long-term safety beyond 12 weeks not well established
dosage.special_populations: Insufficient safety data in pregnancy/lactation; avoid if hypercalcemic or have kidney disease

common_side_effects: Mild gastrointestinal upset reported in some studies

medications: Bisphosphonates (bone medications) — potential additive effects, monitor with healthcare provider
Calcium supplements — may have additive effects on bone metabolism
supplements: Vitamin D, calcium, magnesium — may have complementary bone-support effects

name: CistaMAX Optimized Cistanche Complex
summary: Cistanche is a traditional Chinese herb containing phenylethanoid glycosides and iridoid glycosides, traditionally used for vitality and endurance support. Modern research suggests potential benefits for sexual function, energy, and cognitive performance.
evidence_notes: Limited human RCT data; most evidence is preclinical or from traditional use. CistaMAX is a proprietary extract with potential standardization benefits, but clinical validation in humans remains preliminary.

dosage.typical_range: 500-1000 mg daily (extract form varies by standardization)
dosage.with_food: Can be taken with or without food
dosage.duration_notes: Benefits may take 4-8 weeks to manifest; long-term safety in humans not extensively studied
dosage.special_populations: Avoid during pregnancy; limited safety data in children

medications: Potential interaction with phosphodiesterase inhibitors (additive vasodilation)
supplements: Other NO-boosting supplements (L-arginine, L-citrulline) may have additive effects

name: L-Citrulline / Citrulline Malate
summary: Converts to arginine, boosting nitric oxide. Enhances blood flow and reduces fatigue.
evidence_notes: Well-established for performance
mechanism_of_action: Converts to arginine (better than direct arginine)

dosage.special_populations: Athletes, cardiovascular support

optimal_dose.label: 6-8 g L-citrulline OR 8 g citrulline malate

upper_limit.label: No established UL
upper_limit.notes: Mild GI upset at high doses

citations.title: Citrulline and exercise performance
citations.finding: Enhances exercise performance and reduces fatigue

citations.title: Citrulline malate systematic review
citations.finding: Improves high-intensity exercise and reduces soreness

name: L-Citrulline (Blood Pressure)
summary: Converts to arginine, raising NO levels. Better tolerated than arginine for many.
evidence_notes: Sustained NO elevation

dosage.special_populations: Blood pressure, exercise, circulation

name: Citrulline Malate
summary: Citrulline bound to malic acid. The malic acid may add endurance benefits through Krebs cycle support.
evidence_notes: Popular pre-workout ingredient
mechanism_of_action: Citrulline converts to arginine for NO

dosage.special_populations: Athletes wanting pumps/endurance

name: Citrus Bergamot Extract
summary: Citrus bergamot is a citrus fruit rich in flavonoids and polyphenols, primarily studied for potential cardiovascular and lipid-modulating effects. Limited human clinical evidence supports use for cholesterol and triglyceride management, though most research is preliminary.
mechanism_of_action: Flavonoid and polyphenol content may modulate lipid metabolism
evidence_notes: Most human evidence is small, preliminary, or short-duration. Some positive results for lipid profiles in limited RCTs, but insufficient high-quality evidence for strong recommendations. Effect sizes modest when present.

dosage.with_food: Recommended; fat-soluble polyphenols absorb better with food
dosage.duration_notes: Most studies used 8–12 week durations; long-term safety data limited
dosage.special_populations: Pregnant/nursing women should consult healthcare provider; may interact with statin medications

contraindications: Concurrent use with statin drugs (potential synergistic effect; medical supervision recommended)

medications: Statins (HMG-CoA reductase inhibitors) — potential additive lipid-lowering effects; monitor
supplements: Other lipid-modulating herbs (e.g., red yeast rice, plant sterols) — monitor for cumulative effects

name: Citrus Bioflavonoids
summary: Plant compounds from citrus fruits that enhance vitamin C absorption and provide antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and vascular support benefits.
evidence_notes: Synergistic with vitamin C; moderate evidence for vascular health
mechanism_of_action: Enhance vitamin C absorption and retention

dosage.special_populations: Those taking vitamin C supplements

practical_notes: Food sources include citrus pith and pulp

name: Citrus Bioflavonoids Complex
summary: A mixture of plant compounds from citrus fruits including hesperidin, diosmin, and naringin that act as antioxidants and may support vascular health. Commonly used to enhance vitamin C absorption and support capillary integrity.
evidence_notes: Moderate evidence for vascular support and antioxidant benefits, particularly hesperidin. Most research on isolated bioflavonoids rather than complex mixtures.

dosage.with_food: Recommended; taken with vitamin C enhances effect
dosage.special_populations: No known contraindications in healthy populations

common_side_effects: Generally well-tolerated; rare mild GI upset at high doses

supplements: May enhance vitamin C bioavailability and effects

name: CLA (Conjugated Linoleic Acid)
summary: Fatty acid with modest effects on body composition. Reduces body fat while preserving muscle.
evidence_notes: Modest evidence for body composition

dosage.special_populations: Body composition goals

name: Clove Extract
summary: Clove extract is derived from flower buds and contains eugenol as its primary active compound, with traditional uses in oral health and digestive support. Limited clinical evidence exists for efficacy in humans, though in vitro and animal studies suggest potential antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
mechanism_of_action: Free radical scavenging via polyphenol content
evidence_notes: Most evidence is from in vitro and animal studies. Limited peer-reviewed human clinical trials support traditional uses. The 10:1 extract concentration lacks specific human efficacy data; dosing based on traditional usage patterns.

dosage.typical_range: 250-1000 mg daily (extract form varies by concentration)
dosage.with_food: May be taken with food to minimize potential gastric irritation

name: Cocoa Bean Extract
summary: Cocoa bean extract is rich in polyphenols, particularly flavanols, which have been studied for cardiovascular and cognitive benefits. Multiple clinical trials support modest improvements in blood flow, endothelial function, and blood pressure when consumed regularly.
mechanism_of_action: Flavanol-mediated nitric oxide production and endothelial vasodilation
Polyphenol antioxidant activity reducing oxidative stress
Phosphodiesterase inhibition from theobromine and caffeine
evidence_notes: Cocoa flavanols have substantial RCT evidence for cardiovascular and endothelial benefits. Effects on cognition are promising but more moderate. Evidence is strongest for high-flavanol extracts; plain cocoa powder is less standardized.

dosage.typical_range: 100-900 mg daily (as whole extract) or 200-600 mg flavanols daily
dosage.timing: With meals to enhance absorption and minimize stomach upset
dosage.with_food: Recommended; fat intake may enhance polyphenol bioavailability
dosage.duration_notes: Regular daily consumption for 2-12 weeks typical in clinical trials
dosage.special_populations: Generally safe; caffeine-sensitive individuals should consider total caffeine intake from cocoa (12-26 mg per gram cocoa powder)

common_side_effects: Mild jitteriness or insomnia (from caffeine and theobromine)
Possible headaches or migraines in sensitive individuals

medications: CYP1A2 substrates (e.g., theophylline) — potential increase in levels
Antihypertensive medications — additive blood pressure lowering possible
supplements: Other caffeine-containing supplements (guarana, green tea, coffee extract)

name: Cocoa Powder
summary: Cocoa powder is rich in flavonoids, particularly epicatechin, which support cardiovascular health and cognitive function. It contains small amounts of stimulants (caffeine and theobromine) and bioactive compounds with neuroprotective properties.

dosage.with_food: Can be taken with or without food
dosage.duration_notes: Safe for long-term use; effects may accumulate over weeks

supports phosphatidylcholine formation in neural membranes
enhances ATP production in brain mitochondria

name: Colloidal Silver (Nanoparticle)
summary: Colloidal silver consists of microscopic silver particles suspended in liquid, historically marketed for antimicrobial and immune support claims. Current evidence does not support safety or efficacy for oral supplementation, and regulatory agencies have raised significant concerns about bioaccumulation and argyria (skin discoloration).
mechanism_of_action: Proposed silver ion release with antimicrobial activity (in vitro only)
evidence_notes: Very limited human evidence; no robust clinical trials support oral safety or efficacy. FDA and other regulatory bodies have issued warnings against colloidal silver supplements. Primary concerns: bioaccumulation, lack of established safe dosage, and risk of argyria with chronic use.

dosage.typical_range: Typically marketed at 10-500 ppm (parts per million), but no established safe dose
dosage.duration_notes: Chronic use carries risk of argyria; bioaccumulation occurs with repeated dosing
dosage.special_populations: Not recommended for any population; avoid in pregnancy, lactation, and children

contraindications: All populations (not FDA-approved as safe supplement)

medications: May reduce absorption of certain medications including antibiotics and thyroid drugs
supplements: No well-documented interactions, but concurrent mineral supplements may affect absorption

evidence_notes: While myrrh has traditional use spanning centuries and in-vitro/animal studies support anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activity, human clinical trials are sparse. Supercritical CO2 extraction with standardization to furanodienones improves consistency, but evidence-grade remains moderate due to limited RCT data in humans.

name: Copper (as Copper Bisglycinate Chelate)
summary: Copper is an essential trace mineral required for iron metabolism, collagen formation, and antioxidant enzyme function. Copper bisglycinate chelate is a highly absorbable form bound to glycine for optimal bioavailability.
mechanism_of_action: cofactor for cytochrome c oxidase and other metalloenzymes
evidence_notes: Copper has good evidence for bone health, collagen synthesis, and iron metabolism. Bisglycinate chelate form improves absorption and reduces GI irritation compared to copper sulfate or oxide.

dosage.with_food: can be taken with or without food; food may reduce absorption
dosage.duration_notes: ongoing for general health; do not exceed 10 mg daily

name: Copper (as Cupric Oxide)
summary: Copper is an essential trace mineral required for iron metabolism, collagen synthesis, and antioxidant enzyme function. It plays critical roles in energy production and immune function.
primary_uses: Cofactor for cytochrome c oxidase and other metalloenzymes
mechanism_of_action: Required cofactor for multiple oxidative enzymes
Facilitates electron transfer in the electron transport chain
Essential for cross-linking collagen and elastin
evidence_notes: Copper is an established essential mineral with well-characterized biochemical roles. Deficiency causes documented clinical symptoms; adequacy is supported by extensive metabolic research.

dosage.with_food: Recommended to reduce GI upset and enhance absorption
dosage.duration_notes: Safe for long-term supplementation at recommended levels
dosage.special_populations: Avoid excessive intake in those with Wilson's disease or genetic copper metabolism disorders

common_side_effects: Nausea and GI upset at high doses
contraindications: Cholestasis or liver disease affecting copper excretion
Concurrent use of penicillamine without medical supervision

Antacids (may impair absorption if taken simultaneously)
supplements: High-dose zinc (may inhibit copper absorption via competitive absorption)

name: Copper (as Fermented Copper Bisglycinate)

mechanism_of_action: Involved in iron oxidation and ferroxidase activity
evidence_notes: Established physiological role in pregnancy; RCT evidence supporting copper adequacy during pregnancy; deficiency linked to poor fetal outcomes; bisglycinate form confirmed safe and bioavailable.

dosage.with_food: take with food to enhance absorption and minimize GI irritation; separate from high-dose zinc by at least 2 hours

evidence_notes: Essential for energy and heart

dosage.special_populations: Statin users, heart patients, over 40

name: CoQ10 (Ubiquinol)
summary: Ubiquinol is the active, reduced form of CoQ10, a fat-soluble antioxidant essential for mitochondrial ATP production and cellular energy metabolism. It supports heart health, cellular energy, and may reduce statin-related side effects.
mechanism_of_action: Essential cofactor in the electron transport chain (Complex I–III) for ATP synthesis
Direct free radical scavenger and antioxidant
Regenerates vitamin E and other antioxidants
Supports endothelial function and vascular health
evidence_notes: Strong evidence for cardiovascular benefit (especially heart failure and hypertension); well-established ATP production role; ubiquinol form shows superior bioavailability vs. ubiquinone; statin interaction supported by multiple RCTs.

dosage.timing: With meals (fat-soluble; requires dietary fat for absorption)
dosage.with_food: Essential; take with a meal containing fat for optimal bioavailability
dosage.duration_notes: Effects build over 4–12 weeks; long-term use is safe and appropriate
dosage.special_populations: Statin users: 100–200 mg daily recommended to replenish depleted levels; elderly may benefit from higher doses due to age-related decline

contraindications: Caution in blood-clotting disorders (minor vitamin K-like activity)

medications: Statins – statins deplete CoQ10; supplementation recommended for long-term statin users
Blood thinners (warfarin, anticoagulants) – monitor, as CoQ10 has minor vitamin K-like effects
Antidiabetic medications – CoQ10 may enhance glucose control; monitor blood sugar

name: Cordyceps Militaris
summary: Cordyceps militaris is a cultured fungus traditionally used in Chinese medicine to support energy and endurance, with emerging research suggesting potential benefits for athletic performance and oxygen utilization. The 10:1 extract with standardized cordycepin content provides a concentrated source of bioactive compounds.
mechanism_of_action: May increase ATP production and cellular energy
Cordycepin and adenosine may enhance oxygen utilization
evidence_notes: Cordyceps militaris has some human studies showing modest improvements in exercise performance and lactate clearance, but the evidence base is limited compared to major performance compounds. Most studies are small or conducted in specific athletic populations. Cultivated C. militaris is more accessible than wild C. sinensis but has less robust evidence.

dosage.timing: Morning or 30–60 minutes before exercise for performance use
dosage.with_food: Can be taken with or without food; many prefer with food to minimize GI sensitivity

Those on immunosuppressant medications (cordyceps may modulate immune function)

Blood thinners (theoretical concern; monitor with providers)

name: Cordyceps militaris Mycelium

Research suggests potential benefits for oxygen utilization and endurance, though human evidence is limited and often involves cultivated mycelium rather than wild-harvested fruiting bodies.

Bioactive compounds including cordycepin and polysaccharides
evidence_notes: Limited human RCT evidence exists for Cordyceps militaris specifically. Some small studies show modest improvements in aerobic capacity and endurance, but sample sizes are small and results are mixed. Mycelium-on-grain products lack robust clinical validation compared to whole fruiting bodies. Evidence is preliminary and insufficient for strong recommendation.

dosage.with_food: May be taken with or without food
dosage.duration_notes: Typically used continuously; some evidence suggests benefits emerge after 2-4 weeks of consistent use

name: Docosahexaenoic Acid (DHA)
summary: DHA is a long-chain omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acid critical for brain structure and function, particularly in the cerebral cortex and retina. It is one of the most abundant fatty acids in the brain and supports cognitive health, mood, and visual function.
mechanism_of_action: Incorporates into neuronal membranes, supporting synaptic plasticity
evidence_notes: Extensive RCT evidence supports DHA's role in brain health, cognitive function, and mood. Multiple large studies (VITAL, OmegAD trials) demonstrate cognitive benefits in aging populations. Strong evidence for visual and cardiovascular health.

dosage.timing: With meals (fat-soluble; better absorption with food)

supplements: Vitamin E: may enhance antioxidant effects

evidence_notes: Moderate-to-good human evidence for specific outcomes (sexual function, mood, bone density) with mixed results. Well-studied compared to most hormonal supplements, but individual responses vary significantly.

dosage.with_food: Take with fat-containing food for absorption
dosage.duration_notes: Effects typically apparent within 2-4 weeks; most long-term studies used 6-12 months. Hormone levels should be monitored periodically.
dosage.special_populations: Women may use lower doses (10-25 mg); avoid in pregnancy and lactation; use with caution in hormone-sensitive conditions

name: Eicosapentaenoic Acid (EPA)
summary: EPA is a long-chain omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acid with potent anti-inflammatory and mood-supporting properties. It plays a key role in cardiovascular health, mental health, and immune regulation.
primary_uses: Mood and mental health support (depression, anxiety)
mechanism_of_action: Inhibits pro-inflammatory eicosanoid production (IL-6, TNF-α, CRP)
Supports endothelial function and vascular health
Reduces platelet aggregation and blood viscosity
Promotes resolution of inflammation via specialized mediators (resolvins, protectins)
evidence_notes: Strong RCT evidence for mood support (numerous trials for depression); consistent evidence for cardiovascular markers (triglycerides, inflammation). FDA-approved Vascepa (high-dose EPA) for hypertriglyceridemia in prescription setting demonstrates clinical efficacy.

dosage.timing: With meals (fat-soluble; better absorption with food)
dosage.with_food: Recommended with a meal containing dietary fat
dosage.duration_notes: Benefits for mood typically emerge within 4–8 weeks; cardiovascular benefits may take 8–12 weeks; safe for long-term use
dosage.special_populations: Depression support: 1,000–2,000 mg/day effective in trials; cardiovascular patients: doses up to 4,000 mg/day studied; pregnancy: standard dose recommended for maternal omega-3 status

contraindications: Bleeding disorders or concurrent anticoagulant/antiplatelet therapy (high doses may increase bleeding risk)
Seafood/fish allergy (algae-derived EPA is safe alternative)

medications: Anticoagulants (warfarin, dabigatran): may potentiate blood-thinning at high doses
Antiplatelet agents (aspirin, clopidogrel): additive antiplatelet effects
Antidepressants: no direct interactions; may provide synergistic mood support
supplements: DHA: synergistic effects; commonly combined in fish oil products
Vitamin E: may enhance antioxidant effects

name: Epicatechin
summary: Cocoa flavanol that may inhibit myostatin, the muscle growth limiter. Supports muscle development and blood flow.
evidence_notes: Promising mechanism, limited clinical data in humans

dosage.special_populations: Muscle building, natural athletes

name: Epicatechin (90% (-)-Epicatechin from Camellia sinensis)
summary: Epicatechin is a plant-derived polyphenol and flavanol found abundantly in green tea and cocoa, known for potent antioxidant and vascular effects. Research suggests it may support endothelial function, cardiovascular health, and exercise performance.
evidence_notes: Solid human and animal evidence for vascular and cardiovascular effects; multiple RCTs support NO-mediated improvements in blood flow and endothelial function; less robust evidence for cognitive enhancement alone, though used in neurostack formulations.

dosage.timing: With meals for absorption; timing relative to exercise varies by study
dosage.with_food: May be taken with or without food; may enhance absorption with fat or carbohydrates
dosage.duration_notes: Most studies assess 4-12 weeks; chronic supplementation appears safe
dosage.special_populations: Generally recognized as safe in pregnancy; no major age restrictions

common_side_effects: Rare: mild GI upset at high doses
contraindications: Avoid if allergic to tea/cocoa plants
Use cautiously with bleeding disorders (mild anticoagulant activity)

supplements: Other polyphenols (additive antioxidant effects, generally beneficial)

name: EpiCor® (Saccharomyces cerevisiae Fermentate)
summary: EpiCor is a fermented baker's yeast (Saccharomyces cerevisiae) whole-cell extract studied primarily for immune support and antioxidant effects. Clinical evidence is moderate and primarily limited to specific proprietary formulations, with benefits most established for respiratory health and innate immune markers.
mechanism_of_action: Modulation of innate immunity via pattern recognition receptors
Antioxidant activity from phenolic compounds and metabolites
Potential enhancement of natural killer cell and neutrophil function
evidence_notes: Moderate preliminary evidence from several RCTs showing benefits for respiratory symptoms and immune markers; however, studies are mostly industry-sponsored, limited in scope, and use the proprietary EpiCor formulation specifically, limiting generalizability to other S. cerevisiae fermentates. Evidence grade does not reach B due to small sample sizes and narrow clinical endpoints.

dosage.timing: Can be taken with or without meals; consistent daily timing recommended
dosage.with_food: Not required; may be taken with or without food
dosage.duration_notes: Studies typically use 8-12 weeks; longer-term safety data limited
dosage.special_populations: Generally recognized as safe; limited data in pregnancy, nursing, or immunocompromised individuals

common_side_effects: Rare: allergic reactions in yeast-sensitive individuals
contraindications: Confirmed Saccharomyces cerevisiae allergy or intolerance
Severe immunocompromise (theoretical caution with immune-stimulating compounds)
Caution with candida or active yeast infections (consult healthcare provider)

supplements: No major known interactions; may have additive effects with other immune-supporting ingredients

name: Epimedium brevicornum Extract (Leaf) - 10% Icariin
summary: A traditional Chinese herb extract standardized to 10% icariin, a prenylated flavonol compound used for sexual function and vascular health. Icariin acts as a phosphodiesterase-5 (PDE-5) inhibitor.
mechanism_of_action: PDE-5 inhibition (similar to pharmaceutical ED drugs)
evidence_notes: In vivo and clinical studies support PDE-5 inhibition and erectile function improvement; human RCT data is moderate but emerging. 10% icariin is a meaningful standardization level.

dosage.typical_range: 500-1000 mg daily of 10% icariin extract (providing ~50-100 mg icariin)
dosage.timing: Typically taken 30-60 minutes before sexual activity, or daily for chronic use
dosage.with_food: May take with or without food; fatty meals may enhance absorption
dosage.duration_notes: Acute effects noted within 1-2 hours; chronic benefits develop over 2-4 weeks of daily use
dosage.special_populations: Caution in individuals taking nitrates or other ED medications; monitor blood pressure

contraindications: Concurrent use with nitrates or phosphodiesterase inhibitors (sildenafil, etc.)

supplements: Other vasodilatory supplements (L-citrulline, L-arginine) - may have additive effects

name: Epsom Salt (Magnesium Sulfate)
summary: Transdermal magnesium source via bath. Relaxes muscles, debated absorption.
evidence_notes: Absorption debated but subjectively helpful

dosage.special_populations: Muscle tension, relaxation ritual

name: Ergothioneine
summary: Longevity amino acid with unique cellular uptake via dedicated transporter. Potent antioxidant concentrated in mitochondria, potential longevity compound.
evidence_notes: Emerging longevity research, dedicated transporter suggests importance

dosage.special_populations: Longevity-focused, antioxidant support

practical_notes: Body has dedicated transporter suggesting importance

name: Eurycomax Optimized Tongkat Ali Complex
summary: A standardized Tongkat Ali extract marketed for testosterone support and male vitality. Contains bioactive compounds including quassinoids and glycoproteins traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine.
mechanism_of_action: May enhance testosterone production via LH signaling
evidence_notes: Multiple RCTs show modest improvements in testosterone levels and sexual function in men; Eurycomax is a proprietary standardized extract with published research support. Evidence is solid but effect sizes are moderate.

dosage.duration_notes: Benefits may take 2-4 weeks to manifest; studies typically evaluate 8-12 week duration
dosage.special_populations: Not recommended for women or individuals sensitive to hormonal effects

common_side_effects: Mild insomnia (if taken late in day)

medications: May interact with blood pressure medications
Possible interaction with testosterone replacement therapy
supplements: Other testosterone-supporting herbs should be combined cautiously

name: Evening Primrose
summary: GLA-rich oil traditionally used for PMS, breast tenderness, and women's health.
evidence_notes: Mixed evidence, may help PMS breast symptoms

dosage.special_populations: Women with PMS, cyclic breast tenderness

practical_notes: May help breast tenderness more than other PMS
May help skin and breast symptoms

name: Evening Primrose Oil (PMS)
summary: GLA-rich oil traditionally used for PMS breast tenderness and hormonal symptoms.
evidence_notes: Mixed evidence, traditionally used for PMS breast symptoms

dosage.special_populations: PMS, especially breast tenderness

name: Extra Virgin Coconut Oil
summary: Extra virgin coconut oil is a plant-based fat rich in medium-chain triglycerides (MCTs) and saturated fatty acids, commonly used for energy support and general wellness. Limited clinical evidence supports many popular health claims, though some research suggests potential metabolic and cognitive benefits.
mechanism_of_action: Medium-chain triglycerides may be more readily absorbed and metabolized for energy than long-chain fats
Potential effects on satiety and thermogenesis
evidence_notes: While coconut oil is well-tolerated and contains bioactive MCTs, evidence for specific health benefits in humans is limited and mixed. Some studies show modest metabolic effects, but claims about cognitive enhancement, weight loss, and disease prevention lack strong RCT support.

dosage.timing: Can be taken anytime; some prefer with meals
dosage.with_food: May be better tolerated with food; can be consumed straight or added to beverages
dosage.special_populations: May cause digestive upset in sensitive individuals; start with lower doses

common_side_effects: Digestive upset or loose stools (especially at higher doses)
contraindications: May increase lipid levels in susceptible individuals (high in saturated fat)

medications: May theoretically affect fat-soluble medication absorption

name: Fadogia Agrestis
summary: Nigerian shrub gaining popularity for testosterone. Limited human research, animal studies promising.
evidence_notes: Very limited human research, animal studies exist

dosage.special_populations: Experimental/biohacker

serious_warnings: Testicular toxicity in high-dose animal studies

name: Fadogia Agrestis
summary: African herb gaining popularity for testosterone support, though human evidence is limited.
evidence_notes: Limited human evidence, animal studies promising
mechanism_of_action: May increase LH and testosterone (animal studies)

dosage.duration_notes: Cycle: 8 weeks on, 4 weeks off recommended
dosage.special_populations: Men seeking testosterone support

serious_warnings: Potential testicular toxicity at high doses (animal)

name: Fasoracetam
summary: Racetam upregulating GABA-B receptors. May help with GABA tolerance and attention.
evidence_notes: GABA-B upregulation unique

dosage.special_populations: Phenibut withdrawal, attention

name: Fennel (Digestive/Carminative)
summary: Traditional carminative herb for gas, bloating, and colic with mild estrogenic properties.
evidence_notes: Traditional use with supporting evidence

dosage.typical_range: 1-2 tsp seeds as tea or 300-600mg extract
dosage.special_populations: Bloating, gas, infant colic (diluted oil massage)

safety.pregnancy_breastfeeding: Traditional galactagogue but caution with estrogenic effects

name: Fenugreek
summary: Seed extract that may support testosterone and libido. Also supports blood sugar.
evidence_notes: Moderate evidence for testosterone and libido

dosage.special_populations: Men, blood sugar support

name: Fenugreek (Libido)
summary: Herb with evidence for libido enhancement in both men and women.
evidence_notes: Good evidence for libido in both sexes

dosage.special_populations: Both sexes seeking libido support

practical_notes: Testofen for men, Libifem for women

name: Trigonella foenum-graecum Extract (Fenugreek Seed)
summary: Fenugreek seed extract contains alkaloids (trigonelline) and steroidal saponins (protodioscin) with traditional use in supporting metabolic health and hormonal balance. Evidence suggests modest benefits for blood glucose regulation and lactation support, though human clinical data remains limited.
mechanism_of_action: Trigonelline acts as a monoamine oxidase inhibitor and may influence neurotransmitter metabolism
Protodioscin and other saponins may modulate hormone receptor activity
Galactomannan fiber content supports satiety and glucose modulation
Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects from polyphenol compounds
evidence_notes: Fenugreek has moderate evidence for glucose control and lactation in human trials, but most research involves whole seed rather than standardized extracts. The specific 3% trigonelline / 1% protodioscin extract lacks robust RCT validation. Evidence is preliminary for hormonal claims.

dosage.timing: With meals to minimize GI upset
dosage.with_food: Recommended; may reduce nausea and improve absorption
dosage.duration_notes: Safe for long-term use; most studies assess 8–12 weeks for efficacy
dosage.special_populations: Pregnant women should consult healthcare provider before use; generally recognized as safe for lactation support

common_side_effects: Hypoglycemia in susceptible individuals when combined with antidiabetic drugs
contraindications: Pregnancy (traditionally avoided due to uterotonic properties)
Allergy to legumes (fenugreek is a legume)

medications: Antidiabetic drugs (metformin, sulfonylureas) — may have additive hypoglycemic effects
Anticoagulants/antiplatelet agents — potential additive effects (limited evidence)
supplements: Other blood-glucose-lowering herbs (e.g., cinnamon, bitter melon)

name: Fertility Support Stack (Female)
summary: Comprehensive female fertility: CoQ10, Folate, Vitamin D, Inositol, Omega-3, antioxidants.
evidence_notes: Evidence-based comprehensive fertility support

dosage.typical_range: CoQ10 200-400mg + Folate 800mcg + D3 2000-4000IU + Inositol 2-4g (if PCOS) + Omega-3 1-2g + Antioxidants
dosage.special_populations: Women trying to conceive, IVF preparation, age-related fertility

serious_warnings: Not a substitute for medical fertility care
safety.pregnancy_breastfeeding: Designed for preconception, modify at positive test

name: Soluble Fiber (Heart Focus)
summary: Soluble fiber binds cholesterol for excretion. Beta-glucan and psyllium most studied.
evidence_notes: Proven cholesterol reduction

dosage.timing: With meals, with lots of water
dosage.special_populations: High cholesterol, blood sugar support

medications: May reduce absorption of many meds (take 2h apart)

citations.finding: Higher fiber intake associated with lower CVD risk

name: Fisetin

evidence_notes: Potent senolytic flavonoid

dosage.typical_range: 100-500mg daily or high-dose cycling (up to 20mg/kg for senolytic)
dosage.special_populations: Longevity protocols, anti-aging

name: Fish Oil (EPA/DHA)

citations.finding: Fish oil supplementation significantly reduces inflammatory markers

citations.title: Omega-3 and brain health
citations.finding: DHA essential for brain structure and function; deficiency linked to cognitive decline

name: Flaxseed
summary: Flaxseed oil is a plant-based source of alpha-linolenic acid (ALA), an omega-3 fatty acid with modest evidence for cardiovascular and inflammatory support. Human studies show moderate benefits for cholesterol and blood pressure, though conversion to EPA/DHA is limited.
evidence_notes: Good evidence for fiber, lignans, and ALA omega-3 benefits

dosage.special_populations: Digestive support, hormones

practical_notes: Must be ground to absorb nutrients

name: Flaxseed Oil (ALA)
summary: Plant-based omega-3 (ALA) that requires conversion to EPA/DHA. Conversion is inefficient (5-15%). Better than nothing for vegans.
evidence_notes: ALA conversion to EPA/DHA is inefficient

dosage.special_populations: Algal oil better for DHA needs

practical_notes: Algal oil better for brain benefits

name: FloraDLPS Lutein Carotenoid Complex
summary: FloraDLPS is a branded lutein carotenoid complex containing lutein, zeaxanthin, and other carotenoids used to support eye health and macular pigment density. Clinical evidence supports lutein's role in protecting against age-related macular degeneration (AMD) and supporting visual function.
mechanism_of_action: Accumulation in the macula lutea of the eye, filtering blue light and UV radiation
Antioxidant activity protecting retinal cells from oxidative damage
Support of macular pigment optical density (MPOD)
evidence_notes: Lutein has robust evidence from observational studies and the AREDS2 trial showing benefits for AMD prevention and macular health. FloraDLPS specifically is a standardized extract, though most evidence generalizes to lutein/zeaxanthin combinations.

dosage.with_food: Take with fat-containing food to enhance bioavailability
dosage.duration_notes: Long-term daily use; benefits typically observed after 6-12 weeks of consistent supplementation
dosage.special_populations: Generally safe for adults; pregnant/nursing women should consult healthcare provider

supplements: May have additive antioxidant effects with other carotenoid or antioxidant supplements

name: Folate
summary: Essential B vitamin for DNA synthesis, cell division, and methylation. Critical during pregnancy.
evidence_notes: Essential vitamin critical for development

dosage.special_populations: Pregnancy critical, MTHFR variants

name: Folate (as Folic Acid)
summary: Folate is an essential B vitamin critical for DNA synthesis, cell division, and homocysteine metabolism. Folic acid is the synthetic form used in supplements and fortified foods.
mechanism_of_action: Serves as a one-carbon donor in methylation and nucleotide synthesis pathways
Required for DNA replication and repair
Supports conversion of homocysteine to methionine
evidence_notes: Strong RCT evidence supports folate supplementation in pregnancy for neural tube defect prevention. Good evidence for general DNA synthesis and cell division. Homocysteine-lowering effects are well-established.

dosage.typical_range: 200-400 mcg daily (non-pregnant); 400-800 mcg daily (pregnant or planning pregnancy)
dosage.duration_notes: Safe for long-term use; particularly important during pregnancy and lactation
dosage.special_populations: Pregnant/lactating women should use 400-800 mcg daily. Individuals with MTHFR mutations or absorption issues may benefit from methylfolate form.

common_side_effects: High doses (>5000 mcg) may cause bitter taste, nausea
contraindications: Undiagnosed pernicious anemia (B12 deficiency) — high folate may mask symptoms
Certain seizure medications (phenytoin, phenobarbital) — monitor carefully

medications: Methotrexate — antagonistic effect; use folic acid supplementation under medical supervision
Anticonvulsants (phenytoin, phenobarbital, valproate) — may reduce folate absorption
Oral contraceptives — may increase folate requirements
supplements: Vitamin B12 — work synergistically; ensure adequate B12 when supplementing folate

name: Folate (L-5-Methyltetrahydrofolate)
summary: An essential B-vitamin required for DNA synthesis, cell division, and methylation reactions. L-5-methyltetrahydrofolate is the bioactive form and primary circulating form in blood.
mechanism_of_action: one-carbon unit transfer for nucleotide synthesis
evidence_notes: Strong RCT evidence for neural tube defect prevention in pregnancy. 400 mcg daily standard prenatal recommendation from ACOG and CDC.

dosage.duration_notes: essential for preconception and throughout pregnancy and lactation
dosage.special_populations: pregnant women: 400-800 mcg daily (higher doses for MTHFR variants or prior NTD); lactating women: 500 mcg daily

contraindications: avoid high doses in undiagnosed B12 deficiency

supplements: requires adequate B12 for optimal function

name: Methylfolate
summary: Active form of folate for those with MTHFR variants. Critical for methylation and mood.
evidence_notes: Essential nutrient with clear mood connection

dosage.special_populations: MTHFR, depression, pregnancy

practical_notes: Start low if unsure about methylation

citations.title: Methylfolate vs folic acid
citations.finding: Methylfolate more effective in MTHFR polymorphism carriers

citations.title: Folate and depression
citations.finding: Adjunctive methylfolate improves antidepressant response

name: Food Blend
summary: Food blends are concentrations of whole foods or food byproducts designed to provide additional nutrients, cofactors, and phytonutrients in their naturally-occurring forms. They may include nutritional yeast, vegetable powders, fruit concentrates, or fermented ingredients.
mechanism_of_action: May contain natural cofactors enhancing nutrient utilization
Fermentation may increase bioavailability and create metabolites
evidence_notes: Moderate evidence supports the bioavailability benefit of whole-food-derived nutrients and fermented forms compared to synthetic isolates in some contexts. However, most 'food blends' are proprietary with limited transparency; specific efficacy depends on ingredient composition and concentration (typically dilute in multivitamins).

dosage.typical_range: Varies by product; typically 100-500 mg per daily serving in multivitamins
dosage.duration_notes: Ongoing as part of daily nutritional support
dosage.special_populations: Generally safe; individuals with yeast sensitivity should verify if nutritional yeast is included

common_side_effects: Possible allergic reactions if contains known allergens
contraindications: Yeast sensitivity (if blend contains nutritional yeast)
Food allergies to included ingredients (proprietary formulas may not fully disclose all components)

name: Forskolin
summary: Coleus forskohlii extract that increases cAMP. May support fat loss and testosterone.
evidence_notes: Limited but promising evidence

dosage.special_populations: Fat loss, body composition

name: FOS (Fructooligosaccharides)

evidence_notes: Effective prebiotic, may cause gas

dosage.special_populations: FODMAP-sensitive avoid

practical_notes: More gas than inulin or PHGG
Effective but not for sensitive guts

name: FruiteLP Lutein Carotenoid Complex
summary: A branded carotenoid concentrate derived from fruit sources, featuring lutein as a primary component for eye health support. FruiteLP is a proprietary lutein-rich extract marketed for ocular antioxidant protection.
mechanism_of_action: Accumulation in the macula and lens to filter blue light and scavenge free radicals
Protection of retinal pigment epithelium from oxidative damage
evidence_notes: Lutein has good evidence for eye health, supported by the AREDS2 trial and meta-analyses showing benefit in age-related macular degeneration. However, FruiteLP as a specific branded extract has limited independent peer-reviewed validation. Grade reflects strong evidence for lutein itself tempered by proprietary formulation specificity.

dosage.typical_range: 6–20 mg lutein daily (typical range in research; exact amount in this product depends on formulation)
dosage.timing: With meals containing fat for optimal absorption
dosage.duration_notes: Intended for long-term daily use; benefits accumulate over weeks to months
dosage.special_populations: Safe for aging adults; pregnant/nursing women should consult provider

common_side_effects: Carotenemia (yellowing of skin) at very high intakes, though rare at typical supplemental doses
contraindications: No major contraindications; individuals with fruit allergies should verify ingredient sourcing

medications: May interact with cholesterol-lowering drugs (statins) that affect fat absorption, though clinically insignificant at typical doses
supplements: Beneficial with other carotenoids (zeaxanthin, β-carotene) for synergistic eye support

name: Fulvic Acid
summary: Organic compound from humic substances that enhances mineral absorption and transport. Supports nutrient delivery and detoxification.
evidence_notes: Traditional use, mechanism plausible, limited clinical trials

dosage.special_populations: Mineral absorption support

name: Functional Yeast Extract (Saccharomyces cerevisiae) Cell Wall
summary: Functional yeast extract is derived from baker's yeast cell walls and is standardized to contain beta-glucans, polysaccharides that may support immune function. Research suggests beta-glucans can enhance innate immune responses, though human clinical evidence remains limited.
mechanism_of_action: Beta-glucan binding to pattern recognition receptors (Dectin-1) on immune cells
Activation of macrophages and natural killer cells
evidence_notes: Good mechanistic and in vitro evidence for beta-glucan immunomodulation. Some human studies show immune marker improvements (NK cell activity, cytokine production), but limited large-scale RCT data for clinical outcomes. Yeast extract extracts are less studied than purified beta-glucan isolates.

dosage.timing: Can be taken with or without food; consistent daily use recommended for immune benefits
dosage.duration_notes: Intended for regular use; immune effects typically assessed over weeks to months
dosage.special_populations: Generally recognized as safe for most adults; limited safety data in pregnant/nursing women

common_side_effects: Rare: allergic reactions in yeast-sensitive individuals

name: GABA (Supplemental)

dosage.special_populations: Mild anxiety, sleep

name: Ganoderma lucidum Extract (Fruiting Body)
summary: Ganoderma lucidum (reishi) is a medicinal mushroom used traditionally in Asian medicine for stress and immune support. Modern research shows modest evidence for stress reduction and sleep quality, though study quality and standardization remain inconsistent.

Triterpenes (luciderol, ganoderiol) may have sedative and anxiolytic properties
Potential modulation of stress hormones (cortisol) and HPA axis
evidence_notes: Multiple human studies suggest benefits for anxiety and sleep, but methodological quality is often poor, sample sizes modest, and standardization of extracts varies widely. Fruiting body extracts show more promising data than mycelium. Insufficient evidence for strong A or B grade.

dosage.typical_range: 1.5–3 g daily (extract); 1:1 extracts typically 1–3 g daily
dosage.timing: Evening for sleep support; can be taken anytime for general stress management
dosage.with_food: May be taken with or without food; fat may enhance absorption of lipophilic compounds
dosage.duration_notes: Benefits often emerge after 2–4 weeks of consistent use

common_side_effects: Insomnia if taken in excess or too late in day
contraindications: Bleeding disorders (may have mild anticoagulant effects)

supplements: Other adaptogens may have additive effects; monitor for over-stimulation or excess sedation

name: Ganoderma lucidum Extract (Reishi Fruiting Body)

The 8:1 extract ratio indicates concentration of active compounds including polysaccharides and triterpenes.

Triterpenes and peptidoglycans may influence HPA axis and stress response

NSAIDs (additive anti-inflammatory; generally safe but monitor)

name: Ginkgo biloba
summary: Ginkgo biloba leaf extract is a traditional herbal supplement standardized for flavonoid glycosides and terpenoids, with clinical evidence supporting modest benefits for cognitive function and peripheral circulation. The extract has been extensively studied, though effect sizes in healthy populations are modest.
evidence_notes: Improves brain blood flow

dosage.special_populations: Elderly, circulation concerns

serious_warnings: Significant bleeding risk with blood thinners
Avoid with anticoagulants and antiplatelet drugs

Seizure disorders (low-quality extracts may contain ginkgotoxin)

drug_interactions.mechanism: Additive antiplatelet effects + possible CYP interaction

drug_interactions.effect: Increased bleeding risk, including intracranial hemorrhage
Increased bleeding risk; SSRIs also inhibit platelets
May reduce phenytoin levels; seizure risk

safety.interaction_summary: Ginkgo biloba inhibits platelet aggregation and affects CYP enzymes. PRIMARY RISK: Bleeding, especially with anticoagulants, antiplatelet drugs, and NSAIDs. Stop ginkgo 2 weeks before surgery.
safety.interaction_warning: AVOID with blood thinners (warfarin, aspirin, Plavix), before surgery, and with seizure medications. Use caution with SSRIs and diabetes drugs.

name: Ginkgo Biloba (Circulation Focus)
summary: Improves microcirculation, especially to extremities and brain. Traditional circulation herb.

name: GIVOMAG™ Magnesium Glycerophosphate

May enhance intestinal absorption through specific transporter pathways
Provides bioavailable magnesium with reduced GI side effects
evidence_notes: Limited published clinical evidence specific to GIVOMAG™ form. Based on general knowledge of magnesium chelate technology and glycerophosphate carriers showing improved tolerance in preliminary studies. Standard magnesium forms have Grade A evidence; this proprietary form requires more clinical validation.

dosage.timing: With meals to enhance absorption; can be taken morning or evening
dosage.with_food: Take with food to enhance tolerability and absorption

name: Glutathione (Reduced Form)
summary: Reduced glutathione is a tripeptide antioxidant produced endogenously that plays a critical role in cellular detoxification and redox balance. Oral bioavailability is very limited due to degradation in the gastrointestinal tract, making supplementation efficacy questionable for systemic glutathione status.
mechanism_of_action: Acts as a direct free radical scavenger and reduces oxidative stress
Cofactor for glutathione peroxidase and glutathione S-transferase enzymes
evidence_notes: While glutathione's biological role is well-established, oral supplementation has limited evidence for systemic bioavailability and clinical efficacy. Most studies using glutathione were IV or inhaled; oral studies show minimal absorption. Grade C reflects preliminary evidence for oral form specifically.

dosage.timing: Morning on empty stomach may improve absorption slightly
dosage.with_food: With food may reduce absorption; optimal timing unclear due to low bioavailability
dosage.duration_notes: Ongoing use is typical for those seeking antioxidant support; no established maximum duration

contraindications: High doses may theoretically interact with chemotherapy or radiation therapy

medications: Potential interaction with chemotherapy agents (may reduce drug efficacy or increase toxicity)

N-acetylcysteine (NAC) may boost endogenous glutathione synthesis

name: Goji Berry Powder

Limited clinical evidence exists for most marketed health claims, though observational studies suggest potential benefits for eye health and immune function.
mechanism_of_action: Free radical scavenging via phenolic compounds
evidence_notes: Most claims lack robust RCT evidence. Traditional use and preliminary in vitro/animal studies support antioxidant potential, but human clinical trials are limited and often small. Eye health claims (zeaxanthin content) are plausible but not definitively proven in humans.

dosage.timing: Can be taken any time of day; often mixed into beverages or foods

Nightshade sensitivity (goji is in Solanaceae family)

Clinical evidence for efficacy is limited and inconsistent, with most studies examining berberine rather than goldenseal extract directly.

evidence_notes: Limited human clinical trials specific to goldenseal root. Most evidence is traditional use, in vitro antimicrobial data, or studies on isolated berberine. Lacks robust RCT evidence for primary claimed uses.

Glycine and proline support collagen matrix formation
May support intestinal tight junctions and mucosal integrity
Provides precursors for hyaluronic acid and cartilage proteoglycans

Prebiotic substrate for beneficial bacteria fermentation

Enhanced satiety and reduced appetite signaling
evidence_notes: Good evidence from multiple studies demonstrating prebiotic effect, improved insulin sensitivity, and satiety. Resistant starch is well-characterized mechanistically; human trials support digestive and metabolic benefits.

dosage.timing: With meals or as flour component in recipes

supplements: Other resistant starch or prebiotic sources (additive fermentation)

medications: Antihypertensive medications (excess sodium may reduce efficacy)

name: HMB (Beta-Hydroxy Beta-Methylbutyrate)
summary: Leucine metabolite that may reduce muscle protein breakdown. Most useful during caloric restriction or for beginners.
evidence_notes: Helps prevent muscle loss

dosage.special_populations: Cutting phase, beginners, elderly

citations.title: HMB and muscle preservation
citations.finding: Effective for preventing muscle loss during immobilization

citations.title: HMB ISSN position
citations.finding: May enhance gains in strength and lean mass with training

name: Calcium β-hydroxy β-methylbutyrate monohydrate
summary: HMB is a metabolite of the branched-chain amino acid leucine that may support muscle protein synthesis and reduce muscle breakdown during resistance training and caloric restriction. Research shows modest benefits for lean mass retention and strength gains, particularly in untrained or aging populations.
mechanism_of_action: Activates mTOR signaling for anabolic responses
evidence_notes: Moderate evidence from multiple RCTs showing modest lean mass and strength gains in resistance-trained individuals, untrained persons, and aging populations. Effect sizes are generally small to moderate. Most robust evidence exists for untrained individuals and older adults; benefits in elite athletes less clear.

dosage.timing: Distributed throughout the day; around workouts may be optimal
dosage.with_food: May be taken with or without food; consistency more important than timing
dosage.duration_notes: Benefits typically emerge after 2-4 weeks of consistent use; optimal duration unclear but studies extend to 12+ weeks
dosage.special_populations: Appears safe and potentially beneficial for older adults and untrained individuals; limited evidence in elite/highly trained athletes

common_side_effects: Rare reports of nausea or stomach upset at high doses
contraindications: Caution in individuals with severe kidney disease (metabolite clearance)

supplements: May complement resistance training combined with adequate protein intake
No known adverse interactions with common supplements

name: HMB Free Acid
summary: Fast-absorbing form of HMB (beta-hydroxy beta-methylbutyrate). Reduces muscle breakdown and supports recovery, especially during caloric deficit or intense training.
evidence_notes: Better absorption than HMB-Ca, clinical support for muscle preservation

dosage.with_food: Can be taken without for faster absorption
dosage.duration_notes: Benefits during intense training or deficit
dosage.special_populations: Cutting phases, intense training, older athletes

practical_notes: Free acid absorbs faster than calcium form

name: Holy Basil (Tulsi)

name: Holy Basil (Tulsi) Supercritical Extract

Limited human clinical evidence supports traditional use, though constituent compounds show in vitro bioactivity.
mechanism_of_action: Eugenol and caryophyllene may modulate stress hormone pathways
evidence_notes: Holy basil has moderate traditional use evidence and some human studies on stress/anxiety outcomes, but limited rigorous RCT data specifically on supercritical extracts with this standardization. Eugenol and caryophyllene show in vitro activity but human efficacy data is preliminary.

dosage.with_food: May be taken with or without food; with food may reduce any stomach sensitivity
dosage.duration_notes: Traditionally used long-term; 4-8 weeks typically assessed for adaptogenic effects
dosage.special_populations: Avoid if pregnant/nursing; caution with immunosuppressants or hormone-sensitive conditions

common_side_effects: Rare allergic reactions in sensitive individuals
contraindications: Hormone-sensitive cancers (theoretical concern from traditional use)

Anticoagulants/antiplatelets (eugenol may have mild antiplatelet properties—monitor)

name: Honokiol

evidence_notes: Active compound from magnolia bark

name: Hoodia Gordonii Extract
summary: Hoodia gordonii is a succulent plant traditionally used by San hunter-gatherers to suppress appetite, with preliminary studies suggesting potential appetite-suppressing effects through P57 alkaloid compounds. However, human clinical evidence remains limited and conflicting, with most marketed products lacking adequate standardization.
mechanism_of_action: P57 alkaloid may signal satiety to the hypothalamus
evidence_notes: While some animal and small human studies suggest appetite-suppressing activity, the evidence base is limited and inconsistent. Few well-controlled RCTs in humans; many commercial products lack verified P57 content or standardization. Grade C reflects preliminary but not robust clinical support.

dosage.with_food: May be taken with or without food; some protocols suggest before meals for appetite suppression
dosage.duration_notes: Most studies used 12–16 weeks; long-term safety data limited
dosage.special_populations: Not recommended in pregnancy/lactation; limited safety data in children

contraindications: Concurrent use of appetite suppressants or stimulants

supplements: Other appetite suppressants or stimulant herbs (caffeine, synephrine)

name: Hops
summary: Traditional sleep and anxiety herb from the brewing plant. Most consistently effective when combined with valerian.
evidence_notes: Most positive RCTs use hops + valerian combinations; standalone evidence is limited. Traditional use is extensive.
mechanism_of_action: Bitter acids (humulones, lupulones) have sedative properties in animal models

dosage.duration_notes: No clear cycling needed; safe for ongoing use
dosage.special_populations: Avoid in hormone-sensitive conditions due to phytoestrogen content

safety.pregnancy_breastfeeding: Avoid — insufficient safety data plus phytoestrogen activity
safety.liver_kidney_notes: No significant concerns at typical doses

medications: CNS depressants (benzodiazepines, alcohol, opioids) — additive sedation
supplements: Other sedative herbs (valerian, kava) — additive effect

practical_notes: Strongest evidence is for hops + valerian combinations
Phytoestrogen content is significant — not appropriate for all populations
Older studies suggest a sleep onset latency benefit

name: Hyaluronic Acid
summary: A naturally occurring polysaccharide that holds up to 1000x its weight in water, supporting skin hydration, joint lubrication, and connective tissue health. Sodium hyaluronate is the salt form with enhanced bioavailability.
evidence_notes: Good evidence from multiple RCTs for skin hydration and joint comfort; well-established physiological role. Oral bioavailability improved with low molecular weight forms and sodium salt.

dosage.typical_range: 20-200 mg daily (oral); molecular weight varies by product
dosage.with_food: Can be taken with or without food
dosage.duration_notes: Benefits accumulate over 4-8 weeks; typically requires ongoing use for sustained effects
dosage.special_populations: Safe for most; vegans may prefer non-animal sourced forms (some derived from rooster combs or bacterial fermentation)

supplements: Synergistic with collagen peptides and Vitamin C
Works well with biotin for comprehensive skin support

name: Hyaluronic Acid (Oral)
summary: Key component of synovial fluid and cartilage. Oral supplementation may support joint lubrication.
evidence_notes: Emerging evidence for oral bioavailability

dosage.special_populations: Joint stiffness, dry skin

name: Hydrolyzed Bovine Collagen Peptides
summary: Hydrolyzed bovine collagen peptides are bioavailable protein fragments derived from bovine connective tissue, primarily containing Type I and III collagen. Studies suggest potential benefits for skin elasticity, joint comfort, and connective tissue support, though evidence for structural improvements remains mixed.
mechanism_of_action: Provides bioavailable amino acids (glycine, proline, hydroxyproline) that serve as building blocks for collagen synthesis
May stimulate fibroblast activity and extracellular matrix remodeling
Supports joint cartilage integrity and hydration
evidence_notes: Multiple RCTs show modest improvements in skin elasticity, hydration, and joint pain/mobility. Evidence is stronger for skin health than structural joint repair. Quality studies exist but effect sizes are generally small to moderate.

dosage.timing: Consistent daily dosing; timing with meals not critical
dosage.with_food: Can be taken with or without food; may enhance absorption when paired with vitamin C
dosage.duration_notes: Minimum 8-12 weeks of consistent use recommended to assess skin and joint benefits
dosage.special_populations: Safe in pregnancy; ensure grass-fed/high-quality sourcing; vegans should note bovine source

common_side_effects: Mild digestive symptoms (bloating, heartburn) in sensitive individuals

supplements: Vitamin C: May enhance collagen synthesis when combined
Hyaluronic acid: Complementary for skin hydration benefits
Glycine supplements: Avoid excessive combined intake without medical guidance

name: Hydrolyzed Collagen (Type I & III)
summary: Broken-down collagen peptides for skin, hair, nails, and connective tissue. Most common form.
evidence_notes: Popular, growing evidence

dosage.special_populations: Skin aging, joint support, athletes

practical_notes: Take with vitamin C for synthesis

name: Hydrolyzed Collagen (Bovine)
summary: Hydrolyzed bovine collagen is a processed form of collagen broken down into smaller peptides for improved absorption and bioavailability. Emerging evidence suggests potential benefits for joint health, skin elasticity, and connective tissue support, though human studies remain limited.
mechanism_of_action: Provides bioavailable amino acids (glycine, proline, hydroxyproline) that serve as building blocks for collagen synthesis
May stimulate fibroblast activity and Type I and III collagen production
Reduces collagen breakdown through potential anti-inflammatory effects
evidence_notes: Moderate evidence from several human RCTs showing improvements in joint pain, skin hydration, and elasticity. Multiple well-designed studies support efficacy, though larger and longer-duration trials would strengthen evidence. Grade B reflects consistent positive data with adequate sample sizes in key studies.

dosage.timing: Can be taken anytime; often mixed into beverages or consumed post-workout
dosage.with_food: Can be taken with or without food; absorption may be enhanced with vitamin C intake
dosage.duration_notes: Consistent daily intake for 8–12 weeks typically needed to observe benefits; effects are cumulative
dosage.special_populations: Generally safe for most adults; those with allergies to beef should avoid

common_side_effects: Generally well-tolerated; rare digestive upset in sensitive individuals
Mild bloating or heartburn in some users
contraindications: Concerns about mad cow disease (sourcing from reputable suppliers minimizes risk)
Religious or dietary restrictions prohibiting beef products

supplements: Vitamin C: May enhance collagen synthesis when taken together
Hyaluronic Acid: Complementary for skin hydration
Glucosamine/Chondroitin: May have additive joint-support effects

name: Hydrolyzed Collagen (Chicken)
summary: Hydrolyzed chicken collagen is a bioavailable source of amino acids (primarily glycine, proline, and hydroxyproline) derived from chicken connective tissue. Limited research exists specifically on chicken collagen; most evidence applies to bovine or marine collagen for joint health and skin elasticity.
mechanism_of_action: Provides amino acid building blocks for collagen synthesis
May stimulate endogenous collagen production via bioactive peptides
evidence_notes: Most robust evidence exists for bovine and marine collagen (Grade B). Chicken collagen is less studied; available evidence is preliminary and often in animal models or small human trials. Bioavailability is likely high due to hydrolysis, but clinical efficacy data specific to chicken source is limited.

dosage.timing: Typically taken once daily, often with breakfast or post-workout
dosage.with_food: May be taken with or without food; some evidence suggests timing with vitamin C sources may support collagen synthesis
dosage.duration_notes: Consistent use over 8-12 weeks may be needed to observe benefits for skin and joint health
dosage.special_populations: Safe during pregnancy and lactation; suitable for most ages; no adjustment needed for renal or hepatic impairment at typical doses

common_side_effects: Aftertaste or mild bloating with high doses
contraindications: Avian flu concerns (theoretical, products are processed/heated)

supplements: May complement vitamin C supplementation (supports collagen cross-linking)
May complement hyaluronic acid or MSM for synergistic joint/skin effects

name: Hydrolyzed Collagen (Fish)
summary: Hydrolyzed fish collagen is a bioavailable source of amino acids (primarily glycine, proline, and hydroxyproline) derived from fish skin and bones that may support skin elasticity, joint health, and connective tissue integrity. Evidence from human studies shows modest benefits for skin hydration and joint comfort, though effect sizes are generally small to moderate.
mechanism_of_action: Provides bioavailable dipeptides and tripeptides that cross intestinal barrier intact
Accumulates in skin and cartilage tissues
Stimulates endogenous collagen synthesis via signaling pathways
Supplies amino acid precursors for collagen and elastin production
evidence_notes: Multiple RCTs show statistically significant but modest improvements in skin hydration, elasticity, and joint comfort. Fish-derived collagen has better bioavailability than bovine sources. Grade B reflects consistent human trial data, though clinical effect sizes are modest and study quality varies.

dosage.timing: Can be taken any time; some evidence suggests morning or with vitamin C may optimize absorption
dosage.with_food: May be taken with or without food; taking with vitamin C may enhance collagen synthesis
dosage.duration_notes: Studies typically run 8–12 weeks; benefits may take 4–8 weeks to become noticeable
dosage.special_populations: Generally safe in pregnancy/lactation; Muslim consumers may prefer fish sources over bovine for halal compliance

common_side_effects: Aftertaste or unpleasant flavor in some formulations
Rare allergic reactions in fish-sensitive individuals
contraindications: May not be suitable for strict vegans/vegetarians
No established contraindications with medications at typical doses

supplements: Vitamin C: May enhance collagen synthesis; often combined intentionally
Hyaluronic acid: Complementary for skin hydration; often used together

name: Hydrolyzed Collagen Peptides
summary: Hydrolyzed collagen peptides are bioavailable collagen fragments that may support skin elasticity, joint health, and connective tissue integrity. Evidence supports benefits for skin hydration and joint function, though marine sources are less studied than bovine counterparts.
mechanism_of_action: Provides bioavailable amino acids (glycine, proline, hydroxyproline) for collagen synthesis
May stimulate fibroblast activity and collagen production
evidence_notes: Good evidence exists for hydrolyzed collagen peptides on skin hydration and elasticity from multiple RCTs; joint support evidence is moderate but promising. Marine-specific sources have less clinical data than bovine collagen, warranting B rather than A grade.

dosage.timing: Consistent daily intake; timing relative to meals is not critical
dosage.with_food: May be taken with or without food; taking with vitamin C may support collagen synthesis
dosage.duration_notes: Benefits typically observed after 4-12 weeks of consistent use
dosage.special_populations: Safe for most adults; pregnant/nursing women should consult healthcare provider; pescatarians/vegetarians may have concerns with fish sourcing
